general.g_text: 최근 동물 대신 식물을 키우려는 사람들이 늘고 있다. 동물은 돌보려면 많은 시간과 비용이 드는 데 비해 식물은 키우기도 쉽고 실내 공기도 깨끗하게 해주기 때문이다. 또한 식물은 이웃과 (    ) 지내는 많은 현대인들에게 외로움을 달래 주거나 정서적으로 위안을 주기도 한다. 이처럼 식물을 삶의 동반자로 생각하는 사람들이 늘면서 ‘반려식물’이라는 신조어까지 생겼다.

g_text_translate.vi: Gần đây, ngày càng nhiều người trồng thực vật thay vì nuôi động vật. Bởi vì việc chăm sóc vật nuôi tốn nhiều thời gian và tiền bạc, nhưng còn cây thì dễ trồng và còn giúp làm sạch không khí trong nhà. Thực vật cũng xoa dịu sự cô đơn hoặc mang lại cảm giác thoải mái về tinh thần cho nhiều người hiện đại sống ( ) với những người hàng xóm. Khi ngày càng có nhiều người coi thực vật như những người bạn đồng hành trong cuộc sống của họ, một từ mới gọi là "thực vật đồng hành" đã xuất hiện.
g_text_translate.en: Recently, more and more people are growing plants instead of raising animals. Because taking care of pets takes a lot of time and money, but plants are easy to grow and also help clean the air in the house. Plants also soothe loneliness or provide emotional comfort to many modern people who build walls with their neighbors. As more and more people consider plants as companions in their lives, a new word called "companion plants" has emerged.
g_text_translate.fr:
Récemment, de plus en plus de gens cultivent des plantes au lieu d'élever des animaux. Parce que s'occuper des animaux de compagnie prend beaucoup de temps et d'argent, mais les plantes sont faciles à cultiver et contribuent également à assainir l'air de la maison. Les plantes apaisent également la solitude ou apportent un réconfort émotionnel à de nombreuses personnes modernes qui construisent des murs avec leurs voisins. Alors que de plus en plus de gens considèrent les plantes comme des compagnons dans leur vie, un nouveau mot appelé « plantes compagnes » est apparu.

#######

Recently, more and more people are growing plants instead of raising animals. Because taking care of pets takes a lot of time and money, but plants are easy to grow and also help clean the air in the house. Plants also soothe loneliness or provide emotional comfort to many modern people who build walls with their neighbors. As more and more people consider plants as companions in their lives, a new word called "companion plants" has emerged.
g_text_translate.es:
Recientemente, más y más personas cultivan plantas en lugar de criar animales. Porque cuidar a las mascotas requiere mucho tiempo y dinero, pero las plantas son fáciles de cultivar y también ayudan a limpiar el aire de la casa. Las plantas también alivian la soledad o brindan consuelo emocional a muchas personas modernas que construyen muros con sus vecinos. A medida que más y más personas consideran a las plantas como compañeras en sus vidas, ha surgido una nueva palabra llamada "plantas compañeras".

#######

Recently, more and more people are growing plants instead of raising animals. Because taking care of pets takes a lot of time and money, but plants are easy to grow and also help clean the air in the house. Plants also soothe loneliness or provide emotional comfort to many modern people who build walls with their neighbors. As more and more people consider plants as companions in their lives, a new word called "companion plants" has emerged.
g_text_translate.id:
Baru-baru ini, semakin banyak orang menanam tanaman daripada memelihara hewan. Karena merawat hewan peliharaan membutuhkan banyak waktu dan uang, tetapi tanaman mudah tumbuh dan juga membantu membersihkan udara di dalam rumah. Tanaman juga menenangkan kesepian atau memberikan kenyamanan emosional bagi banyak orang modern yang membangun tembok dengan tetangga mereka. Karena semakin banyak orang menganggap tanaman sebagai sahabat dalam hidup mereka, sebuah kata baru yang disebut "tanaman pendamping" telah muncul.

#######

Recently, more and more people are growing plants instead of raising animals. Because taking care of pets takes a lot of time and money, but plants are easy to grow and also help clean the air in the house. Plants also soothe loneliness or provide emotional comfort to many modern people who build walls with their neighbors. As more and more people consider plants as companions in their lives, a new word called "companion plants" has emerged.
g_text_translate.ja:
最近では、動物を飼う代わりに植物を育てる人が増えています。ペットの世話は時間もお金もかかりますが、植物は育てやすく、家の中の空気をきれいにしてくれます。植物はまた、隣人と壁を作る多くの現代人に孤独を和らげたり、心の安らぎを与えてくれます。植物を生活のパートナーと考え​​る人が増えるにつれ、「コンパニオン プラント」という新しい言葉が生まれました。

#######

Recently, more and more people are growing plants instead of raising animals. Because taking care of pets takes a lot of time and money, but plants are easy to grow and also help clean the air in the house. Plants also soothe loneliness or provide emotional comfort to many modern people who build walls with their neighbors. As more and more people consider plants as companions in their lives, a new word called "companion plants" has emerged.
g_text_translate.de:
In letzter Zeit bauen immer mehr Menschen Pflanzen an, anstatt Tiere zu züchten. Denn die Pflege von Haustieren kostet viel Zeit und Geld, aber Pflanzen sind einfach zu züchten und helfen auch, die Luft im Haus zu reinigen. Pflanzen lindern auch die Einsamkeit oder bieten vielen modernen Menschen, die mit ihren Nachbarn Mauern bauen, emotionalen Trost. Da immer mehr Menschen Pflanzen als Begleiter in ihrem Leben betrachten, ist ein neues Wort namens "Begleitpflanzen" entstanden.

#######

Plants also soothe loneliness or provide emotional comfort to many modern people who build walls with their neighbors. As more and more people consider plants as companions in their lives, a new word called "companion plants" has emerged.
g_text_translate.zh-CN:
最近，越来越多的人开始种植植物而不是饲养动物。因为照顾宠物需要花费大量的时间和金钱，但植物很容易生长，也有助于清洁屋内的空气。植物还可以缓解孤独感或为许多与邻居筑墙的现代人提供情感上的安慰。随着越来越多的人把植物当做生活中的伴侣，一个新词出现了，叫做“伴侣植物”。

Di recente, sempre più persone coltivano piante invece di allevare animali. Perché prendersi cura degli animali domestici richiede molto tempo e denaro, ma le piante sono facili da coltivare e aiutano anche a pulire l'aria in casa. Le piante leniscono anche la solitudine o forniscono conforto emotivo a molte persone moderne che costruiscono muri con i loro vicini. Poiché sempre più persone considerano le piante come compagne della loro vita, è emersa una nuova parola chiamata "piante da compagnia".

g_text_translate.ru:
В последнее время все больше и больше людей выращивают растения вместо того, чтобы разводить животных. Потому что уход за домашними животными отнимает много времени и денег, а растения легко выращивать, а также помогают очищать воздух в доме. Растения также успокаивают одиночество или обеспечивают эмоциональный комфорт многим современным людям, которые строят стены со своими соседями. Поскольку все больше и больше людей считают растения спутниками своей жизни, появилось новое слово под названием «растения-компаньоны».

ช่วงนี้ผู้คนหันมาปลูกพืชแทนการเลี้ยงสัตว์มากขึ้นเรื่อยๆ เพราะการดูแลสัตว์เลี้ยงต้องใช้เวลาและเงินมากแต่ต้นไม้ก็โตง่ายและยังช่วยฟอกอากาศในบ้านอีกด้วย พืชยังช่วยบรรเทาความเหงาหรือมอบความสบายใจให้กับคนสมัยใหม่จำนวนมากที่สร้างกำแพงร่วมกับเพื่อนบ้าน ในขณะที่ผู้คนจำนวนมากขึ้นมองว่าพืชเป็นเพื่อนในชีวิตของพวกเขา คำใหม่ที่เรียกว่า "พืชสหาย" ก็เกิดขึ้น

हाल ही में, अधिक से अधिक लोग जानवरों को पालने के बजाय पौधे उगा रहे हैं। क्योंकि पालतू जानवरों की देखभाल में बहुत समय और पैसा लगता है, लेकिन पौधे उगाने में आसान होते हैं और घर में हवा को साफ करने में भी मदद करते हैं। पौधे अकेलेपन को भी शांत करते हैं या कई आधुनिक लोगों को भावनात्मक आराम प्रदान करते हैं जो अपने पड़ोसियों के साथ दीवारें बनाते हैं। जैसे-जैसे अधिक से अधिक लोग पौधों को अपने जीवन में साथी के रूप में मानते हैं, "साथी पौधे" नामक एक नया शब्द सामने आया है।

Son zamanlarda giderek daha fazla insan hayvan yetiştirmek yerine bitki yetiştiriyor. Çünkü evcil hayvanların bakımı çok fazla zaman ve para gerektirir, ancak bitkilerin yetiştirilmesi kolaydır ve aynı zamanda evdeki havanın temizlenmesine de yardımcı olur. Bitkiler ayrıca komşularıyla duvar ören birçok modern insana yalnızlığı yatıştırır veya duygusal rahatlık sağlar. Giderek daha fazla insan bitkileri hayatlarında yoldaş olarak gördükçe, "yoldaş bitkiler" adı verilen yeni bir kelime ortaya çıktı.

4. It is good to raise pets for emotional stability.

4. Il est bon d'élever des animaux de compagnie pour la stabilité émotionnelle.

general.g_text: 인터넷으로 회원 가입을 할 때 설정하는 비밀번호는 초기에는 숫자 네 개면 충분했다. 하지만 최근에는 보안 강화를 위해 특수 문자까지 넣어 만들어야 한다. (   ) 비밀번호 변경도 주기적으로 해야 한다. 이 때문에 가입자는 번거로운 것은 물론이고 자주 바뀌는 비밀번호를 기억하지 못해 스트레스를 받는다. 개인 정보 보호를 가입자에게만 요구하지 말고 기업도 보안 기술 개발에 적극 투자해야 한다.

g_text_translate.vi: Ban đầu, bốn con số là đủ cho mật khẩu đặt khi đăng ký thành viên qua Internet. Tuy nhiên, trong những năm gần đây, các ký tự đặc biệt phải được thêm vào để tăng cường bảo mật. (   ) mật khẩu cũng phải được thay đổi theo định kỳ. Chính vì vậy, những người đăng ký không chỉ gặp phiền phức mà còn căng thẳng vì không nhớ được mật khẩu thường xuyên thay đổi. Không chỉ yêu cầu những người đăng ký bảo vệ thông tin cá nhân, các công ty cũng nên tích cực đầu tư phát triển các công nghệ bảo mật.
g_text_translate.en: Initially, four numbers were sufficient for the password set when registering as a member through the Internet. However, recently, special characters have to be added to enhance security. In addition, you should change your password regularly. Because of this, registrants are not only cumbersome but also stressed because they cannot remember frequently changed passwords. Not only should the protection of personal information be required of registrants, but companies should also actively invest in the development of security technologies.
g_text_translate.fr:
Initialement, quatre chiffres suffisaient pour le mot de passe défini lors de l'inscription en tant que membre via Internet. Cependant, depuis peu, des caractères spéciaux doivent être ajoutés pour renforcer la sécurité. De plus, vous devez changer votre mot de passe régulièrement. De ce fait, les inscrits sont non seulement encombrants mais aussi stressés car ils ne peuvent pas se souvenir des mots de passe fréquemment changés. Non seulement la protection des renseignements personnels devrait être exigée des inscrits, mais les entreprises devraient également investir activement dans le développement de technologies de sécurité.

#######

Initially, four numbers were sufficient for the password set when registering as a member through the Internet. However, recently, special characters have to be added to enhance security. In addition, you should change your password regularly. Because of this, registrants are not only cumbersome but also stressed because they cannot remember frequently changed passwords. Not only should the protection of personal information be required of registrants, but companies should also actively invest in the development of security technologies.
g_text_translate.es:
Inicialmente, cuatro números eran suficientes para la contraseña establecida al registrarse como miembro a través de Internet. Sin embargo, recientemente, se deben agregar caracteres especiales para mejorar la seguridad. Además, debe cambiar su contraseña periódicamente. Debido a esto, los solicitantes de registro no solo son engorrosos sino que también se estresan porque no pueden recordar las contraseñas que cambian con frecuencia. No solo se debe exigir la protección de la información personal de los registrantes, sino que las empresas también deben invertir activamente en el desarrollo de tecnologías de seguridad.

#######

g_text_translate.hi:
प्रारंभ में, इंटरनेट के माध्यम से सदस्य के रूप में पंजीकरण करते समय पासवर्ड सेट करने के लिए चार नंबर पर्याप्त थे। हालाँकि, हाल ही में सुरक्षा बढ़ाने के लिए विशेष वर्ण जोड़ने पड़े हैं। इसके अलावा, आपको अपना पासवर्ड नियमित रूप से बदलना चाहिए। इस वजह से, पंजीकरण कराने वालों को न केवल परेशानी होती है बल्कि वे तनावग्रस्त भी होते हैं क्योंकि वे बार-बार बदले गए पासवर्ड को याद नहीं रख पाते हैं। न केवल पंजीकरणकर्ताओं की व्यक्तिगत जानकारी की सुरक्षा की आवश्यकता होनी चाहिए, बल्कि कंपनियों को सुरक्षा प्रौद्योगिकियों के विकास में भी सक्रिय रूप से निवेश करना चाहिए।

general.g_text: 어떤 사람들은 연예인을 열광적으로 좋아하는 청소년들을 부정적으로 본다. 학교생활에는 소홀하면서 (   ) 무조건 연예인만 쫓아다닌다고 생각하기 때문이다. 하지만 청소년들이 연예인을 좋아하는 것을 그렇게 부정적으로만 볼 일은 아니다. 취향이 비슷한 친구들을 사귈 수도 있고 또 공부나 입시로 인한 부담감에서 벗어날 수도 있기 때문이다. 그들을 너무 부정적으로만 보지 말고 이해하려는 노력이 필요하다.

g_text_translate.vi: Một số người có cái nhìn tiêu cực về thanh thiếu niên đam mê cuồng nhiệt những người nổi tiếng. Điều này là do họ nghĩ rằng các em đang chểnh mảng việc học và (   ) để theo đuổi những người nổi tiếng một cách vô điều kiện. Tuy nhiên, không nên chỉ có cái nhìn tiêu cực khi thấy giới trẻ thích những người nổi tiếng. Bởi vì các em có thể kết bạn với những người có cùng sở thích và giải phóng bản thân khỏi gánh nặng học tập hay thi tuyển sinh. Chúng ta cần cố gắng hiểu các em hơn là nhìn nhận chúng một cách quá tiêu cực.
g_text_translate.en: Some people have a negative view of teenagers who are passionate about celebrities. This is because they think that those teenagers are ignoring school life, throwing caution to the wind and chasing only celebrities unconditionally. However, it is not so negative to see that young people like celebrities. This is because they can make friends with people who have similar tastes and free themselves from the burden of studying or taking the entrance exam. You need to try to understand them rather than seeing them too negatively.
g_text_translate.fr:
Certaines personnes ont une vision négative des adolescents passionnés par les célébrités. C'est parce qu'ils pensent que ces adolescents ignorent la vie scolaire, font fi de la prudence et ne poursuivent inconditionnellement que les célébrités. Cependant, ce n'est pas si négatif de voir que les jeunes aiment les célébrités. En effet, ils peuvent se lier d'amitié avec des personnes qui ont des goûts similaires et se libérer du fardeau d'étudier ou de passer l'examen d'entrée. Il faut essayer de les comprendre plutôt que de les voir trop négativement.

#######

Some people have a negative view of teenagers who are passionate about celebrities. This is because they think that those teenagers are ignoring school life, throwing caution to the wind and chasing only celebrities unconditionally. However, it is not so negative to see that young people like celebrities. This is because they can make friends with people who have similar tastes and free themselves from the burden of studying or taking the entrance exam. You need to try to understand them rather than seeing them too negatively.
g_text_translate.es:
Algunas personas tienen una visión negativa de los adolescentes apasionados por las celebridades. Esto se debe a que piensan que esos adolescentes están ignorando la vida escolar, dejando de lado la precaución y persiguiendo solo a las celebridades incondicionalmente. Sin embargo, no es tan negativo ver que a los jóvenes les gustan las celebridades. Esto se debe a que pueden entablar amistad con personas que tienen gustos similares y liberarse de la carga de estudiar o tomar el examen de ingreso. Tienes que tratar de entenderlos en lugar de verlos demasiado negativamente.

#######

g_text_translate.id:

Ini karena mereka berpikir bahwa para remaja itu mengabaikan kehidupan sekolah, mengabaikan angin dan hanya mengejar selebriti tanpa syarat. Namun, tidak terlalu negatif untuk melihat bahwa anak muda menyukai selebriti. Hal ini karena mereka dapat berteman dengan orang-orang yang memiliki selera yang sama dan membebaskan diri dari beban belajar atau mengikuti ujian masuk. Anda perlu mencoba memahaminya daripada melihatnya terlalu negatif.

#######

Some people have a negative view of teenagers who are passionate about celebrities. This is because they think that those teenagers are ignoring school life, throwing caution to the wind and chasing only celebrities unconditionally. However, it is not so negative to see that young people like celebrities. This is because they can make friends with people who have similar tastes and free themselves from the burden of studying or taking the entrance exam. You need to try to understand them rather than seeing them too negatively.

4. आपके पास उन किशोरों के बारे में समझने का दिमाग होना चाहिए जो मशहूर हस्तियों को पसंद करते हैं।
------------------------
लेखक बच्चों को बहुत अधिक नकारात्मक रूप से देखने के बजाय उन्हें समझने का प्रयास करने का आह्वान करता है। अतः 4 सही उत्तर है।

#######

Choose the central idea of the paragraph.
1. It is good to broaden personal relationships during adolescence.
2. Even if you like celebrities, it shouldn't affect your record.
3. The burden of the entrance exam should not be excessively placed on the youth.
4. You should have a mind of understanding about teenagers who like celebrities.
--------------------
The writer calls for an effort to understand the children rather than seeing them too negatively. So 4 is the correct answer.
explain.tr:
Paragrafın ana fikrini seçin.
1. Ergenlik döneminde kişisel ilişkileri genişletmek iyidir.
2. Ünlüleri sevseniz bile bu sizin sicilinizi etkilememelidir.
3. Giriş sınavının yükü aşırı derecede gençlerin üzerine yüklenmemelidir.
4. Ünlülerden hoşlanan gençlere anlayışlı davranmalısınız.
--------------------
Yazar, çocukları çok olumsuz görmek yerine onları anlamaya yönelik bir çaba sarf edilmesi çağrısında bulunuyor. Yani doğru cevap 4'tür.

general.g_text: 시각 장애인의 안내견은 주인과 있을 때 행인에게 관심을 두지 않는다. (      ) 안내견이 주인을 남겨 두고 행인에게 다가간다면 이는 주인이 위험에 처해 있다는 뜻이다. 안내견은 주인에게 문제가 발생하면 곧장 주변 사람에게 달려가 도움을 요청하도록 훈련을 받기 때문이다. 안내견이 행인의 주위를 맴돌면 안내견을 따라가 주인의 상태를 확인하고 구조 센터에 연락해야 한다.

g_text_translate.vi: Chó dẫn đường cho người mù không chú ý đến người qua đường khi chúng ở cùng chủ. (   ) nếu một con chó dẫn đường bỏ lại chủ nhân của nó và đến gần một người qua đường, điều đó có nghĩa là người chủ đang gặp nguy hiểm. Điều này là do những chú chó dẫn đường được huấn luyện để chạy đến những người xung quanh và yêu cầu giúp đỡ khi chủ của chúng gặp sự cố. Nếu chó dẫn đường lượn lờ xung quanh người qua đường, bạn cần đi theo chú chó, kiểm tra tình trạng của chủ và liên hệ với trung tâm cứu hộ.
g_text_translate.en: Guide dogs for the blind do not pay attention to passersby when they are with their owners. If a guide dog leaves its owner behind and approaches passers-by, it means that the owner is in danger. This is because guide dogs are trained to run to people around and ask for help when their owners are in trouble. If a guide dog is hovering around a passerby, you need to follow the dog, check the owner's condition and contact the rescue center.
g_text_translate.fr:
Les chiens-guides d'aveugles ne font pas attention aux passants lorsqu'ils sont avec leurs maîtres. Si un chien-guide laisse derrière lui son propriétaire et s'approche des passants, cela signifie que le propriétaire est en danger. En effet, les chiens-guides sont formés pour courir vers les gens autour et demander de l'aide lorsque leurs propriétaires sont en difficulté. Si un chien-guide rôde autour d'un passant, vous devez suivre le chien, vérifier l'état du propriétaire et contacter le centre de secours.

#######

This is because guide dogs are trained to run to people around and ask for help when their owners are in trouble. If a guide dog is hovering around a passerby, you need to follow the dog, check the owner's condition and contact the rescue center.

Si un perro guía deja atrás a su dueño y se acerca a los transeúntes, significa que el dueño está en peligro. Esto se debe a que los perros guía están entrenados para correr hacia las personas y pedir ayuda cuando sus dueños están en problemas. Si un perro guía ronda alrededor de un transeúnte, debe seguir al perro, verificar el estado del propietario y comunicarse con el centro de rescate.

Jika anjing pemandu berkeliaran di sekitar pejalan kaki, Anda harus mengikuti anjing tersebut, memeriksa kondisi pemiliknya, dan menghubungi pusat penyelamatan.

content.q_text: 위 글의 내용과 같은 것을 고르십시오.
q_answer: 안내견이 주인 곁을 떠나는 경우는 없다.
안내견은 문제가 생기면 구조 센터로 달려간다.
안내견이 다가오는 것은 위급한 상황이 생겼다는 뜻이다.
안내견은 항상 주변의 사람들에게 관심을 갖도록 훈련을 받는다.

explain.vi:
Chọn đáp án phù hợp với nội dung của đoạn văn.
1. Không có trường hợp nào mà một con chó dẫn đường sẽ rời khỏi chủ nhân của nó.
2. Chó dẫn đường sẽ chạy đến trung tâm cứu hộ nếu có sự cố.
3. Chó dẫn đường đến gần có nghĩa là một trường hợp khẩn cấp đã xảy ra.
4. Chó dẫn đường luôn được huấn luyện để quan tâm đến những người xung quanh.
--------------------
다가오다: Tiến đến gần
위급하다: Nguy cấp

Chó dẫn đường đến gần những người xung quanh khi chủ của chúng gặp sự cố. Vì vậy 3 là đáp án chính xác.
explain.en:
Choose the answer that matches the content of the paragraph.
1. Guide dogs never leave their owners.
2. The guide dog will run to the rescue center if there is a problem.
3. The approach of the guide dog means that an emergency situation has occurred.
4. Guide dogs are always trained to care about those around them.
--------------------
<g>다가오다:</g> To approach
<g>위급하다:</g> To be critical, to be urgent

Guide dogs approach people around when their owners have problems. So 3 is the correct answer.
explain.fr:
Choisissez la réponse qui correspond au contenu du paragraphe.
1. Les chiens-guides ne quittent jamais leurs propriétaires.
2. Le chien-guide courra au centre de secours en cas de problème.
3. L'approche du chien-guide signifie qu'une situation d'urgence s'est produite.
4. Les chiens-guides sont toujours formés pour prendre soin de ceux qui les entourent.
--------------------

general.g_text: 문자 교육은 빠를수록 좋다고 믿는 부모들이 있다. 이들은 자신의 아이가 또래보다 글자를 더 빨리 깨치기를 바라며 문자 교육에 (    ). 그런데 나이가 어린 아이들은 아직 다양한 능력들이 완전히 발달하지 못해 온몸의 감각을 동원하여 정보를 얻는다. 이 시기에 글자를 읽는 것에 집중하다 보면 다른 감각을 사용할 기회가 줄어 능력이 고르게 발달하는데 어려움이 있을 수 있다.

g_text_translate.vi: Có phụ huynh cho rằng cho con học chữ càng sớm càng tốt. Họ muốn con mình học chữ cái nhanh hơn các bạn cùng lứa tuổi nên họ (   ) với việc học chữ. Tuy nhiên, trẻ nhỏ chưa phát triển đầy đủ các năng lực khác nhau nên chúng thu nhận thông tin bằng cách huy động tất cả các giác quan. Tập trung vào việc đọc chữ cái vào thời điểm này làm giảm cơ hội sử dụng các giác quan khác, từ đó khiến trẻ khó phát triển đồng đều các năng lực.
g_text_translate.en: Some parents believe that the sooner their children learn to read, the better they become. They want their children to learn letters faster than their peers, and they are passionate about literacy education. However, young children have not yet fully developed their different capacities, so they receive information by using all of their senses. Focusing on reading letters at this time reduces the opportunity to use other senses, thereby making it difficult for children to develop abilities evenly.
g_text_translate.fr:
Certains parents pensent que plus tôt leurs enfants apprennent à lire, meilleurs ils deviennent. Ils veulent que leurs enfants apprennent les lettres plus rapidement que leurs pairs et ils sont passionnés par l'alphabétisation. Cependant, les jeunes enfants n'ont pas encore pleinement développé leurs différentes capacités, ils reçoivent donc des informations en utilisant tous leurs sens. Se concentrer sur la lecture des lettres à ce moment réduit la possibilité d'utiliser d'autres sens, ce qui rend difficile pour les enfants de développer leurs capacités de manière uniforme.

#######

Some parents believe that the sooner their children learn to read, the better they become. They want their children to learn letters faster than their peers, and they are passionate about literacy education. However, young children have not yet fully developed their different capacities, so they receive information by using all of their senses. Focusing on reading letters at this time reduces the opportunity to use other senses, thereby making it difficult for children to develop abilities evenly.
g_text_translate.es:
Algunos padres creen que cuanto antes aprendan a leer sus hijos, mejor se volverán. Quieren que sus hijos aprendan las letras más rápido que sus compañeros y les apasiona la alfabetización. Sin embargo, los niños pequeños aún no han desarrollado completamente sus diferentes capacidades, por lo que reciben información utilizando todos sus sentidos. Centrarse en leer letras en este momento reduce la oportunidad de usar otros sentidos, lo que dificulta que los niños desarrollen habilidades de manera uniforme.

#######

Some parents believe that the sooner their children learn to read, the better they become. They want their children to learn letters faster than their peers, and they are passionate about literacy education. However, young children have not yet fully developed their different capacities, so they receive information by using all of their senses. Focusing on reading letters at this time reduces the opportunity to use other senses, thereby making it difficult for children to develop abilities evenly.
g_text_translate.id:
Beberapa orang tua percaya bahwa semakin cepat anak-anak mereka belajar membaca, semakin baik mereka menjadi. Mereka ingin anak-anak mereka belajar huruf lebih cepat daripada teman-teman mereka, dan mereka bersemangat tentang pendidikan keaksaraan. Namun, anak kecil belum sepenuhnya mengembangkan kapasitas yang berbeda, sehingga mereka menerima informasi dengan menggunakan semua indera mereka. Fokus membaca huruf pada saat ini mengurangi kesempatan untuk menggunakan indera lain, sehingga menyulitkan anak untuk mengembangkan kemampuannya secara merata.

#######

Some parents believe that the sooner their children learn to read, the better they become. They want their children to learn letters faster than their peers, and they are passionate about literacy education. However, young children have not yet fully developed their different capacities, so they receive information by using all of their senses. Focusing on reading letters at this time reduces the opportunity to use other senses, thereby making it difficult for children to develop abilities evenly.
g_text_translate.ja:
一部の親は、子供が読むことを学ぶのが早ければ早いほど、上手になると信じています。彼らは、子供たちに同級生よりも早く文字を覚えてもらいたいと考えており、識字教育に情熱を注いでいます。しかし、幼い子どもたちは、さまざまな能力をまだ十分に発達させていないため、すべての感覚を使って情報を受け取ります。この時期に文字を読むことに集中すると、他の感覚を使う機会が減り、子どもの能力が均等に発達しにくくなります。

#######

Some parents believe that the sooner their children learn to read, the better they become. They want their children to learn letters faster than their peers, and they are passionate about literacy education. However, young children have not yet fully developed their different capacities, so they receive information by using all of their senses. Focusing on reading letters at this time reduces the opportunity to use other senses, thereby making it difficult for children to develop abilities evenly.
g_text_translate.de:
Einige Eltern glauben, dass ihre Kinder umso besser werden, je früher sie lesen lernen. Sie möchten, dass ihre Kinder Buchstaben schneller lernen als ihre Altersgenossen, und sie setzen sich leidenschaftlich für die Alphabetisierung ein. Kleine Kinder haben ihre unterschiedlichen Fähigkeiten jedoch noch nicht vollständig entwickelt, sodass sie Informationen mit allen Sinnen aufnehmen. Wenn Sie sich zu diesem Zeitpunkt auf das Lesen von Buchstaben konzentrieren, verringert sich die Möglichkeit, andere Sinne zu nutzen, wodurch es für Kinder schwierig wird, Fähigkeiten gleichmäßig zu entwickeln.

#######

Some parents believe that the sooner their children learn to read, the better they become. They want their children to learn letters faster than their peers, and they are passionate about literacy education. However, young children have not yet fully developed their different capacities, so they receive information by using all of their senses. Focusing on reading letters at this time reduces the opportunity to use other senses, thereby making it difficult for children to develop abilities evenly.
g_text_translate.zh-CN:
一些家长认为，孩子越早学会阅读，他们就会变得越好。他们希望自己的孩子比同龄人更快地学习字母，并且对识字教育充满热情。然而，幼儿还没有完全发展他们的不同能力，所以他们通过使用他们所有的感官来接收信息。此时专注于阅读字母会减少使用其他感官的机会，从而使孩子难以均匀发展能力。

#######

Some parents believe that the sooner their children learn to read, the better they become. They want their children to learn letters faster than their peers, and they are passionate about literacy education. However, young children have not yet fully developed their different capacities, so they receive information by using all of their senses. Focusing on reading letters at this time reduces the opportunity to use other senses, thereby making it difficult for children to develop abilities evenly.
g_text_translate.zh-TW:
一些家長認為，孩子越早學會閱讀，他們就會變得越好。他們希望自己的孩子比同齡人更快地學習字母，並且對識字教育充滿熱情。然而，幼兒還沒有完全發展他們的不同能力，所以他們通過使用他們所有的感官來接收信息。此時專注於閱讀字母會減少使用其他感官的機會，從而使孩子難以均勻發展能力。

#######

Some parents believe that the sooner their children learn to read, the better they become. However, young children have not yet fully developed their different capacities, so they receive information by using all of their senses. Focusing on reading letters at this time reduces the opportunity to use other senses, thereby making it difficult for children to develop abilities evenly.
g_text_translate.it:
Alcuni genitori credono che prima i loro figli imparino a leggere, meglio diventino. Vogliono che i loro figli imparino le lettere più velocemente dei loro coetanei e sono appassionati di alfabetizzazione. Tuttavia, i bambini piccoli non hanno ancora sviluppato completamente le loro diverse capacità, quindi ricevono informazioni utilizzando tutti i loro sensi. Concentrarsi sulla lettura di lettere in questo momento riduce l'opportunità di usare altri sensi, rendendo così difficile per i bambini sviluppare abilità in modo uniforme.

#######

Some parents believe that the sooner their children learn to read, the better they become. They want their children to learn letters faster than their peers, and they are passionate about literacy education. However, young children have not yet fully developed their different capacities, so they receive information by using all of their senses. Focusing on reading letters at this time reduces the opportunity to use other senses, thereby making it difficult for children to develop abilities evenly.
g_text_translate.ru:
Некоторые родители считают, что чем раньше их дети научатся читать, тем лучше они станут. Они хотят, чтобы их дети выучили буквы быстрее, чем их сверстники, и с энтузиазмом относятся к обучению грамоте. Однако маленькие дети еще не полностью развили свои различные способности, поэтому они получают информацию, используя все свои чувства. Сосредоточение внимания на чтении букв в это время снижает возможность использования других органов чувств, тем самым затрудняя равномерное развитие способностей детей.

#######

Some parents believe that the sooner their children learn to read, the better they become. They want their children to learn letters faster than their peers, and they are passionate about literacy education. However, young children have not yet fully developed their different capacities, so they receive information by using all of their senses. Focusing on reading letters at this time reduces the opportunity to use other senses, thereby making it difficult for children to develop abilities evenly.
g_text_translate.th:
พ่อแม่บางคนเชื่อว่ายิ่งลูกเรียนรู้การอ่านเร็วเท่าไร พวกเขาก็จะยิ่งเก่งขึ้นเท่านั้น พวกเขาต้องการให้บุตรหลานเรียนรู้จดหมายได้เร็วกว่าเพื่อนๆ และพวกเขามีความหลงใหลในการศึกษาด้านการอ่านออกเขียนได้ อย่างไรก็ตาม เด็กเล็กยังพัฒนาความสามารถที่แตกต่างกันได้ไม่เต็มที่ จึงได้รับข้อมูลโดยใช้ประสาทสัมผัสทั้งหมด การเพ่งความสนใจไปที่การอ่านจดหมายในเวลานี้จะช่วยลดโอกาสในการใช้ประสาทสัมผัสอื่นๆ ส่งผลให้เด็กพัฒนาความสามารถอย่างเท่าเทียมกันได้ยาก

g_text_translate.pt:
Alguns pais acreditam que quanto mais cedo os filhos aprenderem a ler, melhor eles se tornarão. Eles querem que seus filhos aprendam as letras mais rápido do que seus colegas e são apaixonados pela alfabetização. No entanto, as crianças pequenas ainda não desenvolveram totalmente as suas diferentes capacidades, por isso recebem informações usando todos os seus sentidos. Concentrar-se na leitura de cartas neste momento reduz a oportunidade de usar outros sentidos, dificultando assim que as crianças desenvolvam habilidades de maneira uniforme.

#######

Focusing on reading letters at this time reduces the opportunity to use other senses, thereby making it difficult for children to develop abilities evenly.
g_text_translate.hi:
कुछ माता-पिता मानते हैं कि उनके बच्चे जितनी जल्दी पढ़ना सीखेंगे, वे उतने ही बेहतर बनेंगे। वे चाहते हैं कि उनके बच्चे अपने साथियों की तुलना में तेजी से अक्षर सीखें, और वे साक्षरता शिक्षा के प्रति उत्साही हैं। हालाँकि, छोटे बच्चों ने अभी तक अपनी विभिन्न क्षमताओं को पूरी तरह से विकसित नहीं किया है, इसलिए वे अपनी सभी इंद्रियों का उपयोग करके जानकारी प्राप्त करते हैं। इस समय पत्र पढ़ने पर ध्यान केंद्रित करने से अन्य इंद्रियों का उपयोग करने का अवसर कम हो जाता है, जिससे बच्चों के लिए समान रूप से क्षमताओं का विकास करना मुश्किल हो जाता है।

#######

Focusing on reading letters at this time reduces the opportunity to use other senses, thereby making it difficult for children to develop abilities evenly.
g_text_translate.tr:
Bazı ebeveynler, çocuklarının okumayı ne kadar erken öğrenirse o kadar iyi olacağına inanıyor. Çocuklarının harfleri akranlarından daha hızlı öğrenmesini istiyorlar ve okuma-yazma eğitimine büyük önem veriyorlar. Ancak küçük çocuklar henüz farklı kapasitelerini tam olarak geliştirmedikleri için bilgiyi tüm duyularını kullanarak alırlar. Bu dönemde harfleri okumaya odaklanmak, diğer duyuları kullanma fırsatını azaltır, dolayısıyla çocukların yeteneklerini eşit şekilde geliştirmesini zorlaştırır.

explain.vi:
Chọn suy nghĩ trọng tâm của đoạn văn.
1. Các phương pháp dạy chữ phải đa dạng hóa.
2. Cần dạy chữ để nuôi dưỡng giác quan của trẻ.
3. Dạy chữ sớm có thể cản trở sự phát triển của trẻ.
4. Trẻ em nên học chữ vào thời điểm tương tự nhau.
--------------------
방해하다: Gây phương hại, cản trở

Đoạn văn kết luận việc học chữ sớm sẽ khiến trẻ em khó phát triển đồng đều các năng lực. Vì vậy 3 là đáp án chính xác.
explain.en:
Choose the central idea of the paragraph.
1. Literacy education methods must be diversified.
2. Literacy education is necessary to develop a child's senses.
3. Early literacy education can hinder a child's development.
4. It is good for children to learn letters at the same time as each other.
--------------------
<g>방해하다:</g> To interrupt, to disturb

The paragraph concludes that early literacy education makes it difficult for children to develop abilities evenly. So 3 is the correct answer.
explain.fr:
Choisissez l'idée centrale du paragraphe.
1. Les méthodes d'alphabétisation doivent être diversifiées.
2. L'alphabétisation est nécessaire pour développer les sens d'un enfant.
3. L'alphabétisation précoce peut entraver le développement d'un enfant.
4. Il est bon que les enfants apprennent les lettres en même temps que les autres.
--------------------
<g>방해하다:</g>Interrompre, déranger

Le paragraphe conclut que l'alphabétisation précoce empêche les enfants de développer uniformément leurs capacités. Donc 3 est la bonne réponse.

4. Детям полезно учить буквы одновременно друг с другом.
--------------------
<g>방해하다:</g>Прерывать, мешать

В этом параграфе делается вывод о том, что раннее обучение грамоте затрудняет равномерное развитие способностей детей. Итак, 3 – правильный ответ.

general.g_text: 해파리는 몸의 95%가 물로 구성되어 있어 열량이 낮다. 그래서 해파리를 먹고 사는 동물이 거의 없다고 알려져 있었다. 하지만 새나 팽귄，뱀장어 등 많은 동물들에게 해파리는 좋은 먹잇감이다. 해파리에는 비타민이나 콜라겐 같은 영양 성분이 있기 때문이다. (      ) 해파리는 바다 어디에나 있고 도망치지 않아 사냥하기 쉽기 때문이다.

g_text_translate.vi: Sứa biển được cấu tạo 90% từ nước nên có mức năng lượng thấp. Vậy nên gần như từng không có loài động vật nào ăn sứa biển để sống. Tuy nhiên đối với nhiều động vật như chim, cánh cụt hay cá chình thì sứa là một loại thức ăn ngon. Bởi vì sứa có thành phần dinh dưỡng như vitamin hay collagen. (    ) Bởi vì sứa có khắp nơi dưới biển và không chạy trốn nên rất dễ săn.
g_text_translate.en: Jellyfish are low in calories as their body is made up of 95% water. Therefore, it was known that there are few animals that feed on jellyfish. But for many animals, such as birds, penguins, and eels, jellyfish are good prey. This is because jellyfish contain nutrients such as vitamins and collagen. In addition, jellyfish are ubiquitous in the sea and do not escape, making it easy to hunt.
g_text_translate.fr:
Les méduses sont peu caloriques car leur corps est composé à 95% d'eau. Par conséquent, on savait qu'il y avait peu d'animaux qui se nourrissaient de méduses. Mais pour de nombreux animaux, comme les oiseaux, les pingouins et les anguilles, les méduses sont de bonnes proies. En effet, les méduses contiennent des nutriments tels que des vitamines et du collagène. De plus, les méduses sont omniprésentes dans la mer et ne s'échappent pas, ce qui facilite leur chasse.

#######

Jellyfish are low in calories as their body is made up of 95% water. Therefore, it was known that there are few animals that feed on jellyfish. But for many animals, such as birds, penguins, and eels, jellyfish are good prey. This is because jellyfish contain nutrients such as vitamins and collagen. In addition, jellyfish are ubiquitous in the sea and do not escape, making it easy to hunt.
g_text_translate.es:
Las medusas son bajas en calorías ya que su cuerpo está compuesto en un 95% por agua. Por ello, se supo que son pocos los animales que se alimentan de medusas. Pero para muchos animales, como pájaros, pingüinos y anguilas, las medusas son buenas presas. Esto se debe a que las medusas contienen nutrientes como vitaminas y colágeno. Además, las medusas son omnipresentes en el mar y no escapan, lo que facilita su caza.

#######

In addition, jellyfish are ubiquitous in the sea and do not escape, making it easy to hunt.
g_text_translate.id:
Ubur-ubur rendah kalori karena tubuh mereka terdiri dari 95% air. Oleh karena itu, diketahui bahwa ada beberapa hewan yang memakan ubur-ubur. Tetapi bagi banyak hewan, seperti burung, penguin, dan belut, ubur-ubur adalah mangsa yang baik. Hal ini karena ubur-ubur mengandung nutrisi seperti vitamin dan kolagen. Selain itu, ubur-ubur ada di mana-mana di laut dan tidak melarikan diri, sehingga mudah untuk berburu.

Außerdem sind Quallen im Meer allgegenwärtig und entkommen nicht, was die Jagd erleichtert.

explain.vi:
Chọn đáp án phù hợp với nội dung trên.
1. Sứa  gây hại đến hệ sinh thái biển.
2. Sứa là nguồn tài nguyên thức ăn khó đánh bắt.
3. Sứa đang trở thành thức ăn của nhiều loài động vật. 
4. Sứa được cấu tạo chủ yếu từ collagen. 
-----------------------
먹이: Thức ăn
먹잇감: Con mồi

하지만 새나 팽귄, 뱀장어 등 많은 동물들에게 해파리는 좋은 먹잇감이다: Tuy nhiên đối với nhiều động vật như chim, cánh cụt hay cá chình thì sứa là một loại thức ăn ngon.

<g>하지만 새나 팽귄, 뱀장어 등 많은 동물들에게 해파리는 좋은 먹잇감이다:</g>Ancak kuşlar, penguenler ve yılan balıkları gibi birçok hayvan için denizanası iyi bir avdır.

#######

Choose the answer that matches the content of the paragraph.
1. Jellyfish damage marine ecosystems.
2. Jellyfish are a difficult food resource to catch.
3. Jellyfish are food for many animals.
4. Jellyfish are mostly made up of collagen.
-----------------------
<g>먹이:</g> Food
<g>먹잇감:</g> Prey

<g>하지만 새나 팽귄, 뱀장어 등 많은 동물들에게 해파리는 좋은 먹잇감이다:</g> But for many animals, such as birds, penguins, and eels, jellyfish are good prey.

general.g_text: 내비게이션은 목적지까지 길을 안내해 주는 기기이다. 내비게이션이 없이 낯선 곳에 갔다가 길을 못 찾아 (    ) 본 적이 있는 사람이라면 내비게이션이 얼마나 편리한지 느꼈을 것이다. 그러나 우리의 뇌는 스스로 정보를 찾았을 때 그 정보를 오래 기억하는 특성이 있다. 따라서 지나치게 디지털 기기에만 의존하다 보면 정보를 찾고 기억하는 능력이 점점 줄어들어 결국 그 능력을 사용할 수 없게 될지도 모른다.

g_text_translate.vi: Định vị chỉ đường là một loại máy hướng dẫn đường đi đến mục tiêu. Nếu là người từng đến nơi xa lạ, không tìm được đường nên (    ) thì sẽ cảm nhận thấy định vị chỉ đường tiện lợi đến thế nào. Tuy nhiên, não của chúng ta có đặc điểm là khi tự tìm thông tin, thông tin đó sẽ được ghi nhớ lâu hơn. Vì thế nếu dựa vào thiết bị điện tử quá nhiều, năng lực tìm kiếm thông tin và ghi nhớ sẽ giảm sút và không biết chừng chúng ta không thể sử dụng năng lực đó nữa.
g_text_translate.en: A navigation system is a device that guides you to a destination. Anyone who has ever been to an unfamiliar place without a navigation system and struggled to find a way would have felt how convenient navigation is. However, our brain has the characteristic of remembering information for a long time when it finds information on its own. Therefore, if you rely too much on digital devices, your ability to find and remember information will gradually diminish, and you may end up unable to use that ability.
g_text_translate.fr:
Un système de navigation est un appareil qui vous guide vers une destination. Quiconque s'est déjà rendu dans un endroit inconnu sans système de navigation et a eu du mal à trouver un moyen aurait ressenti à quel point la navigation est pratique. Or, notre cerveau a la particularité de se souvenir longtemps des informations lorsqu'il les trouve par lui-même. Par conséquent, si vous comptez trop sur les appareils numériques, votre capacité à trouver et à mémoriser des informations diminuera progressivement et vous risquez de ne plus pouvoir utiliser cette capacité.

#######

A navigation system is a device that guides you to a destination. Anyone who has ever been to an unfamiliar place without a navigation system and struggled to find a way would have felt how convenient navigation is. However, our brain has the characteristic of remembering information for a long time when it finds information on its own. Therefore, if you rely too much on digital devices, your ability to find and remember information will gradually diminish, and you may end up unable to use that ability.
g_text_translate.es:
Un sistema de navegación es un dispositivo que lo guía a un destino. Cualquiera que haya estado alguna vez en un lugar desconocido sin un sistema de navegación y haya tenido problemas para encontrar un camino, habrá sentido lo conveniente que es la navegación. Sin embargo, nuestro cerebro tiene la característica de recordar información durante mucho tiempo cuando encuentra información por sí mismo. Por lo tanto, si confía demasiado en los dispositivos digitales, su capacidad para encontrar y recordar información disminuirá gradualmente y es posible que no pueda utilizar esa capacidad.

#######

A navigation system is a device that guides you to a destination. Anyone who has ever been to an unfamiliar place without a navigation system and struggled to find a way would have felt how convenient navigation is. However, our brain has the characteristic of remembering information for a long time when it finds information on its own. Therefore, if you rely too much on digital devices, your ability to find and remember information will gradually diminish, and you may end up unable to use that ability.
g_text_translate.id:
Sistem navigasi adalah perangkat yang memandu Anda ke suatu tujuan. Siapa pun yang pernah ke tempat asing tanpa sistem navigasi dan berjuang untuk menemukan cara akan merasakan betapa nyamannya navigasi. Namun, otak kita memiliki karakteristik mengingat informasi untuk waktu yang lama ketika menemukan informasi sendiri. Oleh karena itu, jika Anda terlalu mengandalkan perangkat digital, kemampuan Anda untuk menemukan dan mengingat informasi secara bertahap akan berkurang, dan Anda mungkin tidak dapat menggunakan kemampuan itu.

#######

A navigation system is a device that guides you to a destination. Anyone who has ever been to an unfamiliar place without a navigation system and struggled to find a way would have felt how convenient navigation is. However, our brain has the characteristic of remembering information for a long time when it finds information on its own. Therefore, if you rely too much on digital devices, your ability to find and remember information will gradually diminish, and you may end up unable to use that ability.
g_text_translate.ja:
ナビゲーションシステムは、目的地まで案内してくれる装置です。カーナビがなくて見知らぬ場所に行って道に迷ったことがある人なら、カーナビの便利さを実感したことでしょう。しかし、私たちの脳は、自分で情報を見つけた場合、その情報を長く覚えているという特徴があります。そのため、デジタル機器に頼りすぎると、情報を見つけたり覚えたりする能力が徐々に低下し、その能力を使えなくなってしまう可能性があります。

#######

A navigation system is a device that guides you to a destination. However, our brain has the characteristic of remembering information for a long time when it finds information on its own. Therefore, if you rely too much on digital devices, your ability to find and remember information will gradually diminish, and you may end up unable to use that ability.
g_text_translate.de:
Ein Navigationssystem ist ein Gerät, das Sie zu einem Ziel führt. Jeder, der schon einmal ohne Navigationssystem an einem unbekannten Ort war und sich schwer zurechtgefunden hat, wird gespürt haben, wie komfortabel die Navigation ist. Unser Gehirn hat jedoch die Eigenschaft, sich Informationen lange zu merken, wenn es Informationen selbst findet. Wenn Sie sich also zu sehr auf digitale Geräte verlassen, wird Ihre Fähigkeit, Informationen zu finden und sich daran zu erinnern, allmählich nachlassen, und Sie können diese Fähigkeit möglicherweise nicht mehr nutzen.

#######

g_text_translate.zh-CN:
导航系统是引导您到达目的地的设备。任何曾经到过没有导航系统的陌生地方并努力寻找方法的人都会感受到导航的便捷性。然而，我们的大脑在自行查找信息时，具有长时间记忆信息的特点。因此，如果你过度依赖数字设备，你查找和记忆信息的能力就会逐渐减弱，最终你可能无法使用这种能力。

Навигационная система — это устройство, которое ведет вас к месту назначения. g_text_translate.hi:
नेविगेशन सिस्टम एक उपकरण है जो आपको किसी गंतव्य तक मार्गदर्शन करता है। जो कोई भी कभी नेविगेशन प्रणाली के बिना किसी अपरिचित स्थान पर गया हो और रास्ता खोजने के लिए संघर्ष कर रहा हो, उसने महसूस किया होगा कि नेविगेशन कितना सुविधाजनक है। हालाँकि, हमारे मस्तिष्क की विशेषता यह है कि जब वह अपने आप कोई जानकारी खोज लेता है तो वह उसे लंबे समय तक याद रखता है। इसलिए, यदि आप डिजिटल उपकरणों पर बहुत अधिक भरोसा करते हैं, तो जानकारी ढूंढने और याद रखने की आपकी क्षमता धीरे-धीरे कम हो जाएगी, और आप उस क्षमता का उपयोग करने में असमर्थ हो सकते हैं।

Navigasyon sistemi sizi varış noktasına yönlendiren bir cihazdır. Navigasyon sistemi olmadan yabancı bir yere giden ve bir yol bulmakta zorlanan herkes, navigasyonun ne kadar kolay olduğunu hissederdi. Ancak beynimiz kendi kendine bilgi bulduğunda bilgiyi uzun süre hatırlama özelliğine sahiptir. Bu nedenle, dijital cihazlara çok fazla güvenirseniz, bilgiyi bulma ve hatırlama yeteneğiniz giderek azalacak ve bu yeteneğinizi kullanamayacak duruma gelebilirsiniz.

explain.vi:
Chọn suy nghĩ trọng tâm của đoạn văn.
1. Cần có thiết bị điện tử cho cuộc sống tiện lợi.
2. Định vị chỉ đường có tính ứng dụng cao với người lái xe.
3. Phải cố gắng tự tìm thông tin và ghi nhớ.
4. Nếu sử dụng tốt định vị chỉ đường thì sẽ giúp ích cho việc nuôi dưỡng trí nhớ.
--------------------
Bài viết nói rằng nếu lạm dụng các thiết bị như định vị chỉ đường quá nhiều thì khả năng tìm kiếm và ghi nhớ thông tin có thể bị giảm sút, vì vậy nên cố gắng tự tìm kiếm và ghi nhớ thông tin.
explain.en:
Choose the central idea of the paragraph.
1. Digital devices are necessary for a convenient life.
2. Navigation is very useful for drivers.
3. You should make an effort to find and remember information on your own.
4. Good use of navigation helps to improve memory.
--------------------
The paragraph says that if you abuse devices such as navigation too much, your ability to find and remember information may be reduced, so you should try to find and remember information yourself.
explain.fr:
Choisissez l'idée centrale du paragraphe.
1. Les appareils numériques sont nécessaires pour une vie pratique.
2. La navigation est très utile pour les conducteurs.
3. Vous devez vous efforcer de trouver et de mémoriser les informations par vous-même.
4. Une bonne utilisation de la navigation aide à améliorer la mémoire.
--------------------
Le paragraphe indique que si vous abusez trop d'appareils tels que la navigation, votre capacité à trouver et à mémoriser des informations peut être réduite, vous devez donc essayer de trouver et de mémoriser des informations vous-même.

#######

explain.es:
Elige la idea central del párrafo.
1. Los dispositivos digitales son necesarios para una vida cómoda.
2. La navegación es muy útil para los conductores.
3. Debe hacer un esfuerzo para encontrar y recordar información por su cuenta.
4. El buen uso de la navegación ayuda a mejorar la memoria.
--------------------
El párrafo dice que si abusa demasiado de dispositivos como la navegación, su capacidad para encontrar y recordar información puede verse reducida, por lo que debe tratar de encontrar y recordar información usted mismo.

3. Anda harus berusaha mencari dan mengingat informasi sendiri.
4. Penggunaan navigasi yang baik membantu meningkatkan memori.
--------------------
Paragraf tersebut mengatakan bahwa jika Anda menyalahgunakan perangkat seperti navigasi terlalu banyak, kemampuan Anda untuk menemukan dan mengingat informasi mungkin berkurang, jadi Anda harus mencoba mencari dan mengingat informasi itu sendiri.

3. 自分で情報を見つけて覚える努力をするべきです。
4. ナビゲーションを上手に使うと、記憶力が向上します。
--------------------
ナビゲーションなどのデバイスを酷使しすぎると、情報を見つけて覚える能力が低下する可能性があるため、自分で情報を見つけて覚えるようにしてください。

explain.hi:
पैराग्राफ का केंद्रीय विचार चुनें.
1. सुविधाजनक जीवन के लिए डिजिटल उपकरण आवश्यक हैं।
2. नेविगेशन ड्राइवरों के लिए बहुत उपयोगी है।
3. आपको स्वयं जानकारी खोजने और याद रखने का प्रयास करना चाहिए।
4. नेविगेशन का अच्छा उपयोग याददाश्त को बेहतर बनाने में मदद करता है।
------------------------
पैराग्राफ में कहा गया है कि यदि आप नेविगेशन जैसे उपकरणों का बहुत अधिक दुरुपयोग करते हैं, तो जानकारी ढूंढने और याद रखने की आपकी क्षमता कम हो सकती है, इसलिए आपको स्वयं जानकारी ढूंढने और याद रखने का प्रयास करना चाहिए।

3. Bilgiyi kendi başınıza bulup hatırlamaya çalışmalısınız.
4. Navigasyonun iyi kullanımı hafızanın geliştirilmesine yardımcı olur.
--------------------
Paragrafta, navigasyon gibi cihazları çok fazla kötüye kullanırsanız bilgiyi bulma ve hatırlama yeteneğinizin azalabileceği, dolayısıyla bilgiyi kendiniz bulmaya ve hatırlamaya çalışmanız gerektiği belirtiliyor.

general.g_text: 수많은 구직자들이 취업을 위해 최선을 다하지만 취업난으로 인해 합격의 기쁨을 누리는 구직자는 소수에 그치고 있다. 불합격자들은 채용절차의 공정성과 신뢰성 확보를 위해 불합격 사유를 공개할 것을 요구한다. (        ) 회사 측에서는 채용 평가에는 객관화하기 힘든 부분이 많기 때문에 불합격 이유를 구체적으로 알려 주기가 곤란하다고 말하고 있다.

g_text_translate.vi: Nhiều người tìm việc làm hết sức mình để tìm việc, nhưng chỉ có một số người tìm việc tận hưởng được niềm vui khi trúng tuyển, lý do là bởi nạn khó xin việc. Các ứng viên không trúng tuyển yêu cầu tiết lộ lý do họ bị từ chối để đảm bảo tính công bằng và độ tin cậy của quá trình tuyển dụng. (        ) công ty cho biết rất khó đưa ra lý do từ chối một cách cụ thể vì có nhiều phần khó có thể khách quan hóa trong đánh giá tuyển dụng.
g_text_translate.en: Many job seekers do their best to find a job, but only a few job seekers enjoy the joy of passing due to job shortages. Unqualified applicants require companies to disclose the reason for disqualification to ensure fairness and reliability in the recruitment process. On the other hand, companies say that it is difficult to give specific reasons for rejection because there are many parts of the recruitment evaluation that are difficult to objectify.
g_text_translate.it:
Molte persone in cerca di lavoro fanno del loro meglio per trovare un lavoro, ma solo poche persone in cerca di lavoro godono della gioia di passare a causa della carenza di posti di lavoro. I candidati non qualificati richiedono alle aziende di rivelare il motivo della squalifica per garantire equità e affidabilità nel processo di reclutamento. D'altra parte, le aziende affermano che è difficile motivare concretamente il rifiuto perché ci sono molte parti della valutazione delle assunzioni che sono difficili da oggettivare.

#######

Many job seekers do their best to find a job, but only a few job seekers enjoy the joy of passing due to job shortages. Unqualified applicants require companies to disclose the reason for disqualification to ensure fairness and reliability in the recruitment process. On the other hand, companies say that it is difficult to give specific reasons for rejection because there are many parts of the recruitment evaluation that are difficult to objectify.
g_text_translate.fr:
De nombreux demandeurs d'emploi font de leur mieux pour trouver un emploi, mais seuls quelques demandeurs d'emploi profitent de la joie de réussir en raison du manque d'emploi. Les candidats non qualifiés exigent des entreprises qu'elles divulguent le motif de leur disqualification afin de garantir l'équité et la fiabilité du processus de recrutement. D’un autre côté, les entreprises affirment qu’il est difficile de donner des raisons précises de refus car de nombreuses parties de l’évaluation du recrutement sont difficiles à objectiver.

#######

Many job seekers do their best to find a job, but only a few job seekers enjoy the joy of passing due to job shortages. Unqualified applicants require companies to disclose the reason for disqualification to ensure fairness and reliability in the recruitment process. On the other hand, companies say that it is difficult to give specific reasons for rejection because there are many parts of the recruitment evaluation that are difficult to objectify.
g_text_translate.es:
Muchos solicitantes de empleo hacen todo lo posible para encontrar trabajo, pero solo unos pocos disfrutan de la alegría de fallecer debido a la escasez de empleo. Los solicitantes no calificados requieren que las empresas revelen el motivo de la descalificación para garantizar la equidad y confiabilidad en el proceso de contratación. Por otro lado, las empresas dicen que es difícil dar motivos específicos para el rechazo porque hay muchas partes de la evaluación de contratación que son difíciles de objetivar.

#######

Many job seekers do their best to find a job, but only a few job seekers enjoy the joy of passing due to job shortages. Unqualified applicants require companies to disclose the reason for disqualification to ensure fairness and reliability in the recruitment process. On the other hand, companies say that it is difficult to give specific reasons for rejection because there are many parts of the recruitment evaluation that are difficult to objectify.
g_text_translate.id:
Banyak pencari kerja berusaha semaksimal mungkin untuk mendapatkan pekerjaan, namun hanya sedikit pencari kerja yang menikmati nikmatnya lulus karena kekurangan pekerjaan. Pelamar yang tidak memenuhi syarat mengharuskan perusahaan untuk mengungkapkan alasan diskualifikasi untuk memastikan keadilan dan keandalan dalam proses perekrutan. Di sisi lain, perusahaan mengatakan sulit memberikan alasan spesifik penolakan karena banyak bagian evaluasi rekrutmen yang sulit diobjektifkan.

#######

Many job seekers do their best to find a job, but only a few job seekers enjoy the joy of passing due to job shortages. Unqualified applicants require companies to disclose the reason for disqualification to ensure fairness and reliability in the recruitment process. On the other hand, companies say that it is difficult to give specific reasons for rejection because there are many parts of the recruitment evaluation that are difficult to objectify.
g_text_translate.ja:
多くの求職者は就職活動に全力を尽くしますが、就職難により合格の喜びを味わえる求職者はほんの一握りです。採用プロセスの公平性と信頼性を確保するために、不適格な応募者は企業に失格理由の開示を求めます。一方で、採用評価は客観化が難しい部分も多く、企業側からは不採用理由を具体的に示すのが難しいとの声もある。

#######

Many job seekers do their best to find a job, but only a few job seekers enjoy the joy of passing due to job shortages. Unqualified applicants require companies to disclose the reason for disqualification to ensure fairness and reliability in the recruitment process. On the other hand, companies say that it is difficult to give specific reasons for rejection because there are many parts of the recruitment evaluation that are difficult to objectify.
g_text_translate.de:
Viele Arbeitssuchende tun ihr Bestes, um einen Job zu finden, aber aufgrund des Stellenmangels genießen nur wenige Arbeitssuchende die Freude des Ablebens. Unqualifizierte Bewerber verlangen von den Unternehmen, dass sie den Grund für die Disqualifikation offenlegen, um Fairness und Zuverlässigkeit im Einstellungsprozess zu gewährleisten. Andererseits sagen Unternehmen, dass es schwierig sei, konkrete Ablehnungsgründe anzugeben, da viele Teile der Einstellungsbewertung schwer zu objektivieren seien.

#######

Many job seekers do their best to find a job, but only a few job seekers enjoy the joy of passing due to job shortages. Unqualified applicants require companies to disclose the reason for disqualification to ensure fairness and reliability in the recruitment process. On the other hand, companies say that it is difficult to give specific reasons for rejection because there are many parts of the recruitment evaluation that are difficult to objectify.
g_text_translate.zh-CN:
许多求职者竭尽全力寻找工作，但只有少数求职者享受到因工作紧缺而擦身而过的喜悦。对于不合格的应聘者，要求企业披露不合格的原因，以确保招聘过程的公平性和可靠性。另一方面，企业表示，很难给出拒绝的具体原因，因为招聘评估中有很多部分很难客观化。

#######

Many job seekers do their best to find a job, but only a few job seekers enjoy the joy of passing due to job shortages. Unqualified applicants require companies to disclose the reason for disqualification to ensure fairness and reliability in the recruitment process. On the other hand, companies say that it is difficult to give specific reasons for rejection because there are many parts of the recruitment evaluation that are difficult to objectify.
g_text_translate.zh-TW:
許多求職者竭盡全力尋找工作，但只有少數求職者享受到因工作緊缺而擦身而過的喜悅。對於不合格的應聘者，要求企業披露不合格的原因，以確保招聘過程的公平性和可靠性。另一方面，企業表示，很難給出拒絕的具體原因，因為招聘評估中有很多部分很難客觀化。

#######

Many job seekers do their best to find a job, but only a few job seekers enjoy the joy of passing due to job shortages. g_text_translate.ru:
Многие соискатели делают все возможное, чтобы найти работу, но лишь немногие из них получают удовольствие от ее ухода из-за нехватки рабочих мест. Неквалифицированные кандидаты требуют от компаний раскрыть причину дисквалификации, чтобы обеспечить справедливость и надежность процесса найма. С другой стороны, компании говорят, что сложно указать конкретные причины отказа, поскольку существует множество частей оценки при приеме на работу, которые трудно объективизировать.

#######

On the other hand, companies say that it is difficult to give specific reasons for rejection because there are many parts of the recruitment evaluation that are difficult to objectify.
g_text_translate.th:
ผู้หางานจำนวนมากพยายามอย่างดีที่สุดในการหางาน แต่มีผู้หางานเพียงไม่กี่คนเท่านั้นที่เพลิดเพลินกับความสุขของการจากไปเนื่องจากการขาดแคลนงาน ผู้สมัครที่ไม่ผ่านคุณสมบัติกำหนดให้บริษัทต้องเปิดเผยเหตุผลในการตัดสิทธิ์เพื่อให้เกิดความยุติธรรมและความน่าเชื่อถือในกระบวนการสรรหาบุคลากร ในทางกลับกัน บริษัทต่างๆ กล่าวว่าเป็นการยากที่จะให้เหตุผลที่เฉพาะเจาะจงสำหรับการปฏิเสธ เนื่องจากการประเมินผลการสรรหาบุคลากรมีหลายส่วนที่ยากต่อการคัดค้าน

#######

Many job seekers do their best to find a job, but only a few job seekers enjoy the joy of passing due to job shortages. Unqualified applicants require companies to disclose the reason for disqualification to ensure fairness and reliability in the recruitment process. On the other hand, companies say that it is difficult to give specific reasons for rejection because there are many parts of the recruitment evaluation that are difficult to objectify.
g_text_translate.pt:
Muitos candidatos a emprego fazem o possível para encontrar um emprego, mas apenas alguns candidatos desfrutam da alegria de passar devido à escassez de empregos. Os candidatos não qualificados exigem que as empresas divulguem o motivo da desqualificação para garantir justiça e confiabilidade no processo de recrutamento. Por outro lado, as empresas afirmam que é difícil apresentar razões específicas para a rejeição porque há muitas partes da avaliação do recrutamento que são difíceis de objectivar.

#######

Many job seekers do their best to find a job, but only a few job seekers enjoy the joy of passing due to job shortages. Unqualified applicants require companies to disclose the reason for disqualification to ensure fairness and reliability in the recruitment process. On the other hand, companies say that it is difficult to give specific reasons for rejection because there are many parts of the recruitment evaluation that are difficult to objectify.
g_text_translate.hi:
कई नौकरी चाहने वाले नौकरी खोजने की पूरी कोशिश करते हैं, लेकिन केवल कुछ नौकरी चाहने वाले ही नौकरी की कमी के कारण उत्तीर्ण होने की खुशी का आनंद लेते हैं। अयोग्य आवेदकों को भर्ती प्रक्रिया में निष्पक्षता और विश्वसनीयता सुनिश्चित करने के लिए कंपनियों को अयोग्यता के कारण का खुलासा करने की आवश्यकता होती है। दूसरी ओर, कंपनियों का कहना है कि अस्वीकृति के विशिष्ट कारण बताना मुश्किल है क्योंकि भर्ती मूल्यांकन के कई हिस्से ऐसे हैं जिन्हें वस्तुनिष्ठ बनाना मुश्किल है।

#######

g_text_translate.tr:
İş arayanların çoğu iş bulmak için elinden geleni yapıyor, ancak yalnızca birkaç iş arayan iş sıkıntısı nedeniyle geçmenin mutluluğunu yaşıyor. Niteliksiz başvuru sahipleri, işe alım sürecinde adalet ve güvenilirliği sağlamak için şirketlerin diskalifiye nedenini açıklamasını talep eder. Öte yandan şirketler, işe alım değerlendirmesinin nesnelleştirilmesi zor birçok kısmı olduğundan reddedilme için özel nedenler vermenin zor olduğunu söylüyor.

explain.zh-CN:
选择与段落内容相匹配的一项。
1、公司不愿透露取消资格的原因。
2. 成功申请者要求透露录取原因。
3.很少有人在找工作。
4. 尽管职位短缺，但成功申请的人很多。
--------------------
企业表示很难给出拒绝的具体理由，即他们不喜欢透露取消资格的原因。 1 是正确答案。

#######

Choose the one that matches the content of the paragraph.
1. The company is reluctant to disclose the reason for disqualification.
2. Successful applicants ask to disclose the reason for acceptance.
3. Few people are looking for a job.
4. There are many successful applicants despite the job shortage.
--------------------
Companies said it was difficult to give a specific reason for the refusal, that is, they don't like to disclose the reason for disqualification. 1 is the correct answer.

explain.ru:
Выберите тот, который соответствует содержанию абзаца.
1. Компания не желает раскрывать причину дисквалификации.
2. Успешные кандидаты просят указать причину принятия.
3. Мало кто ищет работу.
4. Несмотря на нехватку рабочих мест, есть много успешных претендентов.
--------------------
В компаниях заявили, что затруднились назвать конкретную причину отказа, то есть не любят раскрывать причину дисквалификации. 1 – правильный ответ.

explain.th:
เลือกอันที่ตรงกับเนื้อหาของย่อหน้า
1. บริษัทไม่เต็มใจที่จะเปิดเผยเหตุผลในการตัดสิทธิ์
2. ผู้สมัครที่ประสบความสำเร็จขอให้เปิดเผยเหตุผลในการยอมรับ
3. มีคนไม่กี่คนที่กำลังมองหางาน
4. มีผู้สมัครที่ประสบความสำเร็จจำนวนมากแม้จะขาดแคลนงานก็ตาม
--------------------
บริษัทต่างๆ กล่าวว่าเป็นการยากที่จะให้เหตุผลเฉพาะสำหรับการปฏิเสธ กล่าวคือ พวกเขาไม่ต้องการเปิดเผยเหตุผลในการตัดสิทธิ์ 1 คือคำตอบที่ถูกต้อง

explain.pt:
Escolha aquele que corresponde ao conteúdo do parágrafo.
1. A empresa reluta em divulgar o motivo da desqualificação.
2. Os candidatos aprovados solicitam a divulgação do motivo da aceitação.
3. Poucas pessoas procuram emprego.
4. Existem muitos candidatos aprovados, apesar da escassez de empregos.
--------------------
As empresas disseram que foi difícil dar um motivo específico para a recusa, ou seja, não gostam de divulgar o motivo da desclassificação. 1 é a resposta correta.

explain.hi:
वह चुनें जो अनुच्छेद की सामग्री से मेल खाता हो।
1. कंपनी अयोग्यता के कारण का खुलासा करने में अनिच्छुक है।
2. सफल आवेदक स्वीकृति के कारण का खुलासा करने के लिए कहते हैं।
3. कुछ लोग नौकरी की तलाश में हैं।
4. नौकरी की कमी के बावजूद कई सफल आवेदक हैं।
------------------------
कंपनियों ने कहा कि इनकार का कोई खास कारण बताना मुश्किल है, यानी वे अयोग्यता के कारण का खुलासा करना पसंद नहीं करतीं. 1 सही उत्तर है.

explain.tr:
Paragrafın içeriğine uygun olanı seçin.
1. Şirket diskalifiye nedenini açıklama konusunda isteksizdir.
2. Başarılı başvuru sahiplerinden kabul nedenini açıklamaları istenir.
3. Çok az insan iş arıyor.
4. İş sıkıntısına rağmen pek çok başarılı başvuru sahibi var.
--------------------
Şirketler, ret için spesifik bir neden belirtmenin zor olduğunu, yani diskalifiye sebebini açıklamaktan hoşlanmadıklarını söyledi. 1 doğru cevaptır.

general.g_text: 대형 마트나 백화점의 사은 행사는 구매한 물건 값에 따라 상품권과 생활 용품을 주거나 해외여행 등의 경품에 응모할 수 있도록 하는 방식으로 이루어진다. 소비자의 입장에서는 (        ) 사야 할 물건을 사면서 경제적으로 이익도 되고 생각하지 못했던 경품 당첨의 기회까지 얻을 수 있으니 사은 행사가 나쁘지 않다고 할 수 있다. 그러나 상품권이나 생활 용품 등을 받기 위해서 필요 없는 것까지 구매하고 정작 집에 돌아와서는 후회하는 경우도 많다. 따라서 충동구매를 유도하는 기업들의 유혹에 넘어가는 일이 없도록 현명하게 소비하는 습관을 가져야 한다.

g_text_translate.vi: Các sự kiện tri ân tại các siêu thị lớn và trung tâm thương mại được tổ chức theo cách mà phiếu quà tặng và nhu yếu phẩm hàng ngày được trao theo giá hàng hóa đã mua hoặc mọi người có thể đăng ký nhận quà trúng thưởng như chuyến du lịch nước ngoài. Theo quan điểm của người tiêu dùng, có thể nói, sự kiện tri ân không hề xấu vì nó mang lại lợi ích kinh tế, thậm chí còn mang đến cơ hội trúng giải thưởng bất ngờ trong khi (        ) cũng mua được thứ cần mua. Tuy nhiên, để nhận được phiếu quà tặng hay nhu yếu phẩm hàng ngày, không ít trường hợp mua những thứ không cần dùng đến và tiếc nuối khi về nhà. Vì vậy, cần phải hình thành thói quen tiêu dùng khôn ngoan để không bị rơi vào sự cám dỗ của những doanh nghiệp lôi kéo mua hàng.
g_text_translate.en: Gift events at large marts or department stores are carried out in such a way that gift certificates and daily necessities are given according to the value of purchased goods, or people can apply for prizes such as overseas travel. From the consumer's point of view, a gift event is not a bad thing because you can buy something you need to buy anyway, and you can get an opportunity to win a prize that you didn't expect. However, in order to receive gift certificates or daily necessities, there are many cases where people buy things they do not need and regret it when they return home. Therefore, you should have a habit of spending wisely so that you do not fall for the temptation of companies that induce impulse purchases.
g_text_translate.it:
Gli eventi regalo nei grandi mercati o nei grandi magazzini vengono organizzati in modo tale che i buoni regalo e le necessità quotidiane vengano distribuiti in base al valore dei beni acquistati, oppure le persone possano richiedere premi come viaggi all'estero. Dal punto di vista del consumatore, un evento regalo non è una brutta cosa perché puoi comprare qualcosa che devi comunque comprare e puoi avere l'opportunità di vincere un premio che non ti aspettavi. Tuttavia, per ricevere buoni regalo o beni di prima necessità, ci sono molti casi in cui le persone acquistano cose di cui non hanno bisogno e se ne pentono quando tornano a casa. Pertanto, dovresti avere l’abitudine di spendere saggiamente in modo da non cadere nella tentazione delle aziende che inducono acquisti d’impulso.

#######

Gift events at large marts or department stores are carried out in such a way that gift certificates and daily necessities are given according to the value of purchased goods, or people can apply for prizes such as overseas travel. From the consumer's point of view, a gift event is not a bad thing because you can buy something you need to buy anyway, and you can get an opportunity to win a prize that you didn't expect. However, in order to receive gift certificates or daily necessities, there are many cases where people buy things they do not need and regret it when they return home. Therefore, you should have a habit of spending wisely so that you do not fall for the temptation of companies that induce impulse purchases.
g_text_translate.fr:
Les événements de cadeaux dans les grands magasins ou les grands magasins sont organisés de telle manière que les chèques-cadeaux et les produits de première nécessité sont offerts en fonction de la valeur des produits achetés, ou que les gens peuvent postuler pour des prix tels que des voyages à l'étranger. Du point de vue du consommateur, un événement cadeau n'est pas une mauvaise chose car vous pouvez acheter quelque chose dont vous avez besoin de toute façon et vous pouvez avoir l'opportunité de gagner un prix auquel vous ne vous attendiez pas. Cependant, afin de recevoir des chèques-cadeaux ou des produits de première nécessité, il existe de nombreux cas où les gens achètent des choses dont ils n'ont pas besoin et le regrettent à leur retour chez eux. Par conséquent, vous devez avoir l’habitude de dépenser judicieusement afin de ne pas succomber à la tentation des entreprises qui incitent aux achats impulsifs.

#######

Gift events at large marts or department stores are carried out in such a way that gift certificates and daily necessities are given according to the value of purchased goods, or people can apply for prizes such as overseas travel. From the consumer's point of view, a gift event is not a bad thing because you can buy something you need to buy anyway, and you can get an opportunity to win a prize that you didn't expect. However, in order to receive gift certificates or daily necessities, there are many cases where people buy things they do not need and regret it when they return home. Therefore, you should have a habit of spending wisely so that you do not fall for the temptation of companies that induce impulse purchases.
g_text_translate.es:
Los eventos de obsequios en grandes centros comerciales o grandes almacenes se llevan a cabo de tal manera que los certificados de regalo y los artículos de primera necesidad se entregan de acuerdo con el valor de los bienes comprados, o las personas pueden solicitar premios como viajes al extranjero. Desde el punto de vista del consumidor, un evento de regalo no es malo porque puedes comprar algo que necesitas comprar de todos modos y puedes tener la oportunidad de ganar un premio que no esperabas. Sin embargo, para recibir certificados de regalo o artículos de primera necesidad, hay muchos casos en los que las personas compran cosas que no necesitan y se arrepienten cuando regresan a casa. Por lo tanto, debes tener el hábito de gastar sabiamente para no caer en la tentación de empresas que inducen a las compras impulsivas.

#######

g_text_translate.id:
Acara pemberian hadiah di mart atau department store besar dilakukan sedemikian rupa sehingga sertifikat hadiah dan kebutuhan sehari-hari diberikan sesuai dengan nilai barang yang dibeli, atau masyarakat dapat mengajukan hadiah seperti jalan-jalan ke luar negeri. Dari sudut pandang konsumen, acara pemberian hadiah bukanlah hal yang buruk karena Anda tetap bisa membeli sesuatu yang perlu Anda beli, dan Anda bisa mendapatkan kesempatan untuk memenangkan hadiah yang tidak Anda duga. Namun, untuk mendapatkan sertifikat hadiah atau kebutuhan sehari-hari, banyak kasus dimana orang membeli barang yang tidak dibutuhkan dan menyesalinya saat pulang ke rumah. Oleh karena itu, Anda harus memiliki kebiasaan berbelanja dengan bijak agar tidak tergoda oleh perusahaan yang mendorong pembelian impulsif.

#######

g_text_translate.ja:
大型マートや百貨店などで行われるプレゼントイベントでは、購入金額に応じて商品券や日用品が贈られたり、海外旅行などの懸賞に応募できたりする仕組みが行われている。消費者からすると、プレゼントイベントは、とにかく買わなければいけないものが買えるし、予想していなかった賞品が当たるチャンスも得られるので、悪いことではありません。しかし、商品券や日用品をもらうために、必要のないものまで買ってしまい、帰ってから後悔するケースも少なくありません。したがって、衝動買いを誘発する企業の誘惑に負けないように、賢くお金を使う習慣を身につける必要があります。

#######

g_text_translate.de:
Geschenkveranstaltungen in großen Märkten oder Kaufhäusern werden so durchgeführt, dass Geschenkgutscheine und Artikel des täglichen Bedarfs entsprechend dem Wert der gekauften Waren ausgegeben werden oder sich Menschen um Preise wie Auslandsreisen bewerben können. Aus der Sicht des Verbrauchers ist ein Geschenkereignis keine schlechte Sache, da man dort etwas kaufen kann, was man ohnehin kaufen muss, und die Chance erhält, einen Preis zu gewinnen, mit dem man nicht gerechnet hat. Um Geschenkgutscheine oder Dinge des täglichen Bedarfs zu erhalten, kommt es jedoch häufig vor, dass Menschen Dinge kaufen, die sie nicht benötigen, und es dann bereuen, wenn sie nach Hause zurückkehren. Daher sollten Sie es sich zur Gewohnheit machen, mit Bedacht auszugeben, damit Sie nicht der Versuchung von Unternehmen erliegen, die zu Impulskäufen anregen.

#######

From the consumer's point of view, a gift event is not a bad thing because you can buy something you need to buy anyway, and you can get an opportunity to win a prize that you didn't expect. g_text_translate.zh-CN:
大型卖场或百货公司举办的送礼活动，根据购买商品的价值赠送礼券和生活用品，也可以申请出国旅游等奖品。从消费者的角度来看，送礼活动并不是一件坏事，因为你无论如何都可以买到你需要买的东西，并且你可以获得获得意想不到的奖品的机会。然而，为了收到礼券或生活用品，人们购买了自己不需要的东西，回家后后悔的情况也不少。因此，你应该养成明智消费的习惯，以免被那些诱导冲动购买的公司所诱惑。

#######

g_text_translate.ru:
Подарочные мероприятия в крупных торговых центрах или универмагах проводятся таким образом, что подарочные сертификаты и предметы первой необходимости выдаются в зависимости от стоимости приобретенных товаров, или люди могут подать заявку на призы, например, поездку за границу. С точки зрения потребителя подарочная акция – это неплохо, потому что вы все равно можете купить то, что нужно купить, и можете получить возможность выиграть приз, которого не ожидали. Однако, чтобы получить подарочные сертификаты или предметы первой необходимости, во многих случаях люди покупают ненужные им вещи и сожалеют об этом, когда возвращаются домой. Поэтому вам следует иметь привычку тратить разумно, чтобы не поддаться искушению компаний, которые побуждают к импульсивным покупкам.

#######

g_text_translate.th:
กิจกรรมของขวัญที่มาร์ทขนาดใหญ่หรือห้างสรรพสินค้าจะดำเนินการในลักษณะที่ให้บัตรของขวัญและของใช้ประจำวันตามมูลค่าของสินค้าที่ซื้อ หรือผู้คนสามารถสมัครรับรางวัล เช่น การเดินทางไปต่างประเทศ จากมุมมองของผู้บริโภค กิจกรรมมอบของขวัญไม่ใช่เรื่องเลวร้ายเพราะคุณสามารถซื้อของที่จำเป็นอยู่แล้วได้ และคุณยังมีโอกาสที่จะได้รับรางวัลที่คุณไม่คาดคิดอีกด้วย อย่างไรก็ตาม เพื่อที่จะรับบัตรของขวัญหรือสิ่งของจำเป็นในชีวิตประจำวัน มีหลายกรณีที่ผู้คนซื้อของที่ไม่จำเป็นและเสียใจเมื่อกลับถึงบ้าน ดังนั้นคุณควรมีนิสัยในการใช้จ่ายอย่างชาญฉลาดเพื่อที่คุณจะได้ไม่ตกอยู่ภายใต้การล่อลวงของบริษัทที่กระตุ้นให้เกิดการซื้อแบบแรงกระตุ้น

#######

Gift events at large marts or department stores are carried out in such a way that gift certificates and daily necessities are given according to the value of purchased goods, or people can apply for prizes such as overseas travel. From the consumer's point of view, a gift event is not a bad thing because you can buy something you need to buy anyway, and you can get an opportunity to win a prize that you didn't expect. However, in order to receive gift certificates or daily necessities, there are many cases where people buy things they do not need and regret it when they return home. Therefore, you should have a habit of spending wisely so that you do not fall for the temptation of companies that induce impulse purchases.
g_text_translate.pt:
Os eventos de presentes em grandes mercados ou lojas de departamentos são realizados de forma que os vales-presente e as necessidades diárias sejam entregues de acordo com o valor dos bens adquiridos, ou as pessoas possam solicitar prêmios, como viagens ao exterior. Do ponto de vista do consumidor, um evento de presentes não é uma coisa ruim porque você pode comprar algo que precisa comprar de qualquer maneira e pode ter a oportunidade de ganhar um prêmio que não esperava. Porém, para receber vales-presente ou necessidades diárias, há muitos casos em que as pessoas compram coisas que não precisam e se arrependem quando voltam para casa. Portanto, você deve ter o hábito de gastar com sabedoria para não cair na tentação de empresas que induzem compras por impulso.

g_text_translate.hi:
बड़े मार्ट या डिपार्टमेंट स्टोर में उपहार कार्यक्रम इस तरह से आयोजित किए जाते हैं कि खरीदे गए सामान के मूल्य के अनुसार उपहार प्रमाण पत्र और दैनिक आवश्यकताएं दी जाती हैं, या लोग विदेश यात्रा जैसे पुरस्कारों के लिए आवेदन कर सकते हैं। उपभोक्ता के दृष्टिकोण से, एक उपहार कार्यक्रम कोई बुरी बात नहीं है क्योंकि आप वैसे भी कुछ खरीद सकते हैं जिसे आपको खरीदना है, और आपको एक पुरस्कार जीतने का अवसर मिल सकता है जिसकी आपने उम्मीद नहीं की थी। हालाँकि, उपहार प्रमाण पत्र या दैनिक आवश्यकताएं प्राप्त करने के लिए, ऐसे कई मामले हैं जहां लोग ऐसी चीजें खरीदते हैं जिनकी उन्हें आवश्यकता नहीं होती है और घर लौटने पर पछताते हैं। इसलिए, आपको समझदारी से खर्च करने की आदत होनी चाहिए ताकि आप उन कंपनियों के प्रलोभन में न पड़ें जो आवेगपूर्ण खरीदारी के लिए प्रेरित करती हैं।

#######

g_text_translate.tr:
Büyük marketlerde veya büyük mağazalarda hediye etkinlikleri, satın alınan malların değerine göre hediye sertifikaları ve günlük ihtiyaçların verildiği veya kişilerin yurt dışı seyahat gibi ödüller için başvuruda bulunabileceği şekilde gerçekleştirilir. Tüketici açısından bakıldığında hediye olayı kötü bir şey değil çünkü zaten satın almanız gereken bir şeyi satın alabilir ve beklemediğiniz bir ödülü kazanma fırsatını yakalayabilirsiniz. Ancak hediye çekleri veya günlük ihtiyaçlar almak için insanların ihtiyaç duymadıkları şeyleri satın aldıkları ve eve döndüklerinde pişman oldukları birçok durum vardır. Bu nedenle, anlık satın alımlara neden olan şirketlerin cazibesine kapılmamak için akıllıca harcama alışkanlığına sahip olmalısınız.

general.g_text: 일주일에 배변 횟수가 세 번이 안 되면 변비다. 변비의 평균 발병률은 약 16%이다. 때문에 변비약을 찾는 사람이 많은데 변비약은 오래 먹으면 장의 민감성을 떨어뜨려 (        ) 증상이 악화될 수 있다. 이때는 약을 쓰는 대신, 배꼽 주변을 마사지하면서 따뜻하게 데워 보자. 그러면 배변 횟수가 일주일에 두 번 이상으로 늘어날 수 있다.

g_text_translate.vi: Nếu bạn đi đại tiện ít hơn ba lần trong một tuần, bạn đang bị táo bón. Tỷ lệ mắc táo bón trung bình là khoảng 16%. Vì vậy, nhiều người đang tìm kiếm thuốc táo bón, nhưng dùng thuốc táo bón trong thời gian dài có thể làm giảm độ nhạy cảm của ruột và (        ) khiến các triệu chứng trầm trọng hơn. Trong trường hợp này, thay vì sử dụng thuốc, hãy vừa làm ấm vừa xoa bóp vùng quanh rốn. Điều này có thể làm tăng tần suất đi đại tiện lên hơn hai lần một tuần.
g_text_translate.en: If you have fewer than three bowel movements per week, you are constipated. The average incidence of constipation is about 16%. Because of this, many people are looking for laxatives, but taking laxatives for a long time can reduce the sensitivity of the intestines and worsen symptoms. At this time, instead of using medicine, let's warm it up by massaging the area around the navel. This can increase the number of bowel movements to twice a week or more.
g_text_translate.it:
Se hai meno di tre movimenti intestinali a settimana, sei stitico. L'incidenza media della stitichezza è di circa il 16%. Per questo motivo, molte persone cercano lassativi, ma l'assunzione di lassativi per un lungo periodo può ridurre la sensibilità dell'intestino e peggiorare i sintomi. In questo momento, invece di usare medicine, scaldiamolo massaggiando la zona intorno all'ombelico. Ciò può aumentare il numero di movimenti intestinali fino a due volte a settimana o più.

#######

If you have fewer than three bowel movements per week, you are constipated. The average incidence of constipation is about 16%. Because of this, many people are looking for laxatives, but taking laxatives for a long time can reduce the sensitivity of the intestines and worsen symptoms. At this time, instead of using medicine, let's warm it up by massaging the area around the navel. This can increase the number of bowel movements to twice a week or more.
g_text_translate.fr:
Si vous allez à la selle moins de trois fois par semaine, vous êtes constipé. L'incidence moyenne de la constipation est d'environ 16 %. Pour cette raison, de nombreuses personnes recherchent des laxatifs, mais la prise prolongée de laxatifs peut réduire la sensibilité des intestins et aggraver les symptômes. A ce moment, au lieu d'utiliser des médicaments, réchauffons-le en massant la zone autour du nombril. Cela peut augmenter le nombre de selles à deux fois par semaine ou plus.

#######

En este momento, en lugar de usar medicamento, calentémoslo masajeando el área alrededor del ombligo. Esto puede aumentar la cantidad de deposiciones a dos veces por semana o más.

Kali ini, daripada menggunakan obat, yuk hangatkan dengan memijat area sekitar pusar. Hal ini dapat meningkatkan jumlah buang air besar menjadi dua kali seminggu atau lebih.
