g_text_translate.vi: Khi nuôi cấy vi sinh vật trong phòng thí nghiệm, chúng ta đều biết rằng chúng phát triển tốt cho đến một lúc nào đó, sau một thời gian nhất định, chúng ngừng phát triển do cạn kiệt thức ăn và tích tụ chất thải và cuối cùng chết. Nhân loại cũng không ngoại lệ. Nếu nhân loại mặc kệ ô nhiễm không khí, ô nhiễm nước, ô nhiễm đất - những sản phẩm phụ của các hoạt động sản xuất - thì vấn đề môi trường sẽ vượt ra khỏi ô nhiễm môi trường và dẫn đến tàn phá môi trường. Kết quả tiếp theo là việc (        ).
g_text_translate.en: It is well known to us that when microorganisms are cultured in a laboratory, they grow well until a certain time, then stop growing due to food depletion and waste accumulation and eventually die. Humanity cannot be an exception. If air pollution, water pollution, and soil pollution, which are by-products of production activities, are left unattended, environmental problems will go beyond environmental pollution and lead to environmental destruction. The result after that is obvious.
g_text_translate.it:
Sappiamo bene che quando i microrganismi vengono coltivati ​​in laboratorio, crescono bene fino a un certo periodo, poi smettono di crescere a causa dell'esaurimento del cibo e dell'accumulo di rifiuti e alla fine muoiono. L’umanità non può essere un’eccezione. Se l’inquinamento dell’aria, dell’acqua e del suolo, che sono sottoprodotti delle attività produttive, vengono lasciati incustoditi, i problemi ambientali andranno oltre l’inquinamento ambientale e porteranno alla distruzione dell’ambiente. Il risultato dopo è ovvio.

#######

It is well known to us that when microorganisms are cultured in a laboratory, they grow well until a certain time, then stop growing due to food depletion and waste accumulation and eventually die. Humanity cannot be an exception. If air pollution, water pollution, and soil pollution, which are by-products of production activities, are left unattended, environmental problems will go beyond environmental pollution and lead to environmental destruction. The result after that is obvious.
g_text_translate.fr:
Il est bien connu que lorsque les micro-organismes sont cultivés en laboratoire, ils se développent bien jusqu’à un certain temps, puis cessent de croître en raison de l’épuisement des aliments et de l’accumulation de déchets et finissent par mourir. L'humanité ne peut pas faire exception. Si la pollution de l’air, de l’eau et des sols, qui sont des sous-produits des activités de production, n’est pas prise en compte, les problèmes environnementaux iront au-delà de la pollution environnementale et conduiront à la destruction de l’environnement. Le résultat est évident.

#######

It is well known to us that when microorganisms are cultured in a laboratory, they grow well until a certain time, then stop growing due to food depletion and waste accumulation and eventually die. Humanity cannot be an exception. If air pollution, water pollution, and soil pollution, which are by-products of production activities, are left unattended, environmental problems will go beyond environmental pollution and lead to environmental destruction. The result after that is obvious.
g_text_translate.es:
Sabemos bien que cuando los microorganismos se cultivan en un laboratorio, crecen bien hasta cierto tiempo, luego dejan de crecer debido al agotamiento de los alimentos y la acumulación de desechos y finalmente mueren. La humanidad no puede ser una excepción. Si no se atiende la contaminación del aire, del agua y del suelo, que son subproductos de las actividades productivas, los problemas ambientales irán más allá de la contaminación ambiental y conducirán a la destrucción del medio ambiente. El resultado después de eso es obvio.

#######

It is well known to us that when microorganisms are cultured in a laboratory, they grow well until a certain time, then stop growing due to food depletion and waste accumulation and eventually die. Humanity cannot be an exception. If air pollution, water pollution, and soil pollution, which are by-products of production activities, are left unattended, environmental problems will go beyond environmental pollution and lead to environmental destruction. The result after that is obvious.
g_text_translate.id:
Kita telah mengetahui bahwa ketika mikroorganisme dibiakkan di laboratorium, mereka tumbuh dengan baik sampai waktu tertentu, kemudian berhenti tumbuh karena menipisnya makanan dan penumpukan limbah, dan akhirnya mati. Kemanusiaan tidak bisa menjadi pengecualian. Apabila pencemaran udara, pencemaran air, dan pencemaran tanah, yang merupakan dampak samping dari kegiatan produksi, dibiarkan begitu saja, maka permasalahan lingkungan hidup akan melampaui pencemaran lingkungan hidup dan berujung pada kerusakan lingkungan hidup. Hasil setelah itu terlihat jelas.

#######

It is well known to us that when microorganisms are cultured in a laboratory, they grow well until a certain time, then stop growing due to food depletion and waste accumulation and eventually die. Humanity cannot be an exception. If air pollution, water pollution, and soil pollution, which are by-products of production activities, are left unattended, environmental problems will go beyond environmental pollution and lead to environmental destruction. The result after that is obvious.
g_text_translate.ja:
微生物を実験室で培養すると、ある時点までは順調に増殖しますが、その後、食物の枯渇や老廃物の蓄積により増殖が止まり、最終的には死滅することはよく知られています。人類も例外ではありません。生産活動の副産物である大気汚染、水質汚染、土壌汚染を放置すると、環境問題は環境汚染を超えて環境破壊につながります。その後の結果は明らかです。

#######

It is well known to us that when microorganisms are cultured in a laboratory, they grow well until a certain time, then stop growing due to food depletion and waste accumulation and eventually die. Humanity cannot be an exception. If air pollution, water pollution, and soil pollution, which are by-products of production activities, are left unattended, environmental problems will go beyond environmental pollution and lead to environmental destruction. The result after that is obvious.
g_text_translate.de:
Es ist uns wohlbekannt, dass Mikroorganismen, die in einem Labor gezüchtet werden, bis zu einer bestimmten Zeit gut wachsen, dann aufgrund von Nahrungsmangel und Abfallansammlungen aufhören zu wachsen und schließlich absterben. Die Menschheit kann keine Ausnahme sein. Wenn Luftverschmutzung, Wasserverschmutzung und Bodenverschmutzung, die Nebenprodukte der Produktionstätigkeit sind, unbeachtet bleiben, werden Umweltprobleme über die Umweltverschmutzung hinausgehen und zur Umweltzerstörung führen. Das Ergebnis danach ist offensichtlich.

我们众所周知，当微生物在实验室培养时，它们会生长良好，直到一定时间，然后由于食物耗尽和废物堆积而停止生长，最终死亡。人类也不可能例外。空气污染、水污染、土壤污染这些生产活动的副产品如果不加以治理，环境问题将超越环境污染，导致环境破坏。之后的结果就很明显了。

#######

Humanity cannot be an exception. If air pollution, water pollution, and soil pollution, which are by-products of production activities, are left unattended, environmental problems will go beyond environmental pollution and lead to environmental destruction. The result after that is obvious.
g_text_translate.zh-TW:

#######

g_text_translate.ru:
Нам хорошо известно, что микроорганизмы, культивируемые в лаборатории, до определенного времени хорошо растут, затем перестают расти из-за истощения запасов пищи и накопления отходов и в конечном итоге погибают. Человечество не может быть исключением. Если оставить без внимания загрязнение воздуха, воды и почвы, которые являются побочными продуктами производственной деятельности, экологические проблемы выйдут за рамки загрязнения окружающей среды и приведут к ее разрушению. Результат после этого очевиден.

g_text_translate.th:

#######

g_text_translate.pt:
É bem sabido para nós que quando os microrganismos são cultivados em laboratório, eles crescem bem até certo momento, depois param de crescer devido ao esgotamento dos alimentos e ao acúmulo de resíduos e eventualmente morrem. A humanidade não pode ser uma exceção. Se a poluição do ar, a poluição da água e a poluição do solo, que são subprodutos das actividades de produção, forem deixadas de lado, os problemas ambientais irão além da poluição ambiental e levarão à destruição ambiental. O resultado depois disso é óbvio.

g_text_translate.hi:
यह हम अच्छी तरह से जानते हैं कि जब सूक्ष्मजीवों को प्रयोगशाला में संवर्धित किया जाता है, तो वे एक निश्चित समय तक अच्छी तरह से विकसित होते हैं, फिर भोजन की कमी और अपशिष्ट संचय के कारण बढ़ना बंद कर देते हैं और अंततः मर जाते हैं। मानवता इसका अपवाद नहीं हो सकती. यदि वायु प्रदूषण, जल प्रदूषण और मृदा प्रदूषण, जो उत्पादन गतिविधियों के उप-उत्पाद हैं, पर ध्यान नहीं दिया गया, तो पर्यावरणीय समस्याएं पर्यावरण प्रदूषण से आगे बढ़ जाएंगी और पर्यावरण विनाश का कारण बनेंगी। उसके बाद का परिणाम स्पष्ट है.

İnsanlık bir istisna olamaz. Üretim faaliyetlerinin yan ürünü olan hava kirliliği, su kirliliği ve toprak kirliliği göz ardı edilirse çevre sorunları çevre kirliliğinin ötesine geçerek çevre tahribatına yol açacaktır. explain.en:
Choose the central thought of the paragraph.
1. Humanity must stop production activities to protect the environment.
2. Microorganisms are short-lived when grown in laboratories.
3. Humans, like microorganisms, can survive longer if they live in a natural state.
4. If environmental problems are left unattended, humanity could be endangered.
--------------------
The paragraph concludes with a warning of an obvious result if the environment is destroyed. It can be guessed that the result is that humans are in danger of extinction. 4 is the correct answer.
explain.it:

1. L’umanità deve fermare le attività produttive per proteggere l’ambiente.
2. I microrganismi hanno vita breve se coltivati ​​in laboratorio.
3. Gli esseri umani, come i microrganismi, possono sopravvivere più a lungo se vivono in uno stato naturale.
4. Se i problemi ambientali non vengono presi in considerazione, l’umanità potrebbe essere in pericolo.
--------------------

explain.ja:
段落の中心となる考えを選択します。
1. 人類は環境を守るために生産活動を停止しなければなりません。
2. 微生物は研究室で培養された場合、寿命が短くなります。
3. 人間も微生物と同様、自然な状態で生きていれば長く生存できます。
4. 環境問題を放置すると人類が危険にさらされる可能性があります。
--------------------
この段落は、環境が破壊されれば明らかな結果が生じるという警告で終わります。その結果、人類は絶滅の危機に瀕していると推測できます。 4が正解です。

#######

The paragraph concludes with a warning of an obvious result if the environment is destroyed. It can be guessed that the result is that humans are in danger of extinction. 4 is the correct answer.

--------------------
Der Absatz endet mit einer Warnung vor offensichtlichen Folgen, wenn die Umwelt zerstört wird. Man kann davon ausgehen, dass die Folge ist, dass der Mensch vom Aussterben bedroht ist. 4 ist die richtige Antwort.

general.g_text: 한국은 2000년에 이미 고령 사회에 진입했으며 2030년 정도면 한국 전체 인구 중 노년층의 비율이 30% 이상을 차지할 것으로 예상하고 있다. 노년층은 현재 한국에서 가장 큰 구매자로 (        ). 노인들은 이미 은퇴를 했을 뿐만 아니라 장래가 불안하기 때문에 과소비를 하지 않는 경우가 많다. 또한 활동 범위가 좁아 주로 집 주변에서 식품과 같은 필수품을 구입하는 경향이 있다. 이로 인해 백화점 매출은 줄고 동네 상점, 편의점들의 매출이 늘고 있다. 앞으로 어떤 종류의 사업을 하든, 가장 유력한 구매자인 노인을 고려해야 할 것이다.

g_text_translate.vi: Hàn Quốc đã bước vào xã hội người già vào năm 2000 và đến năm 2030, dự kiến ​​người cao tuổi sẽ chiếm hơn 30% tổng dân số Hàn Quốc. Người cao tuổi hiện (        ) với tư cách là tầng lớp có số lượng người mua lớn nhất ở Hàn Quốc. Những người lớn tuổi thường không chi tiêu quá mức vì họ đã nghỉ hưu và lo lắng về tương lai. Họ cũng có xu hướng mua sắm nhu yếu phẩm, chẳng hạn như thực phẩm, ở quanh nhà là chủ yếu do phạm vi hoạt động hẹp. Kết quả là, doanh thu của các trung tâm thương mại đang giảm và doanh số của các cửa hàng địa phương và cửa hàng tiện lợi ngày càng tăng. Bất kể bạn kinh doanh loại hình nào trong tương lai, bạn sẽ phải xem xét đến người lớn tuổi - những người mua có triển vọng nhất.
g_text_translate.en: Korea has already entered an aged society in 2000, and by 2030, it is expected that the elderly will account for more than 30% of the total population in Korea. The elderly are now in the limelight as the biggest buyers in Korea. Elderly people often do not overspend because they are already retired and their future is uncertain. They also have a narrow range of activities, so they tend to buy necessities such as food mainly around the house. As a result, sales at department stores are declining, while sales at local stores and convenience stores are increasing. No matter what kind of business you do in the future, you will have to consider the most likely buyers: the elderly.
g_text_translate.it:
La Corea è già entrata in una società invecchiata nel 2000 e si prevede che entro il 2030 gli anziani rappresenteranno più del 30% della popolazione totale della Corea. Gli anziani sono ora sotto i riflettori come i maggiori acquirenti in Corea. Gli anziani spesso non spendono troppo perché sono già in pensione e il loro futuro è incerto. Hanno anche una gamma ristretta di attività, quindi tendono ad acquistare beni di prima necessità come il cibo principalmente in casa. Di conseguenza, le vendite nei grandi magazzini stanno diminuendo, mentre le vendite nei negozi locali e nei minimarket sono in aumento. Non importa che tipo di attività farai in futuro, dovrai considerare gli acquirenti più probabili: gli anziani.

#######

Korea has already entered an aged society in 2000, and by 2030, it is expected that the elderly will account for more than 30% of the total population in Korea. The elderly are now in the limelight as the biggest buyers in Korea. Elderly people often do not overspend because they are already retired and their future is uncertain. They also have a narrow range of activities, so they tend to buy necessities such as food mainly around the house. As a result, sales at department stores are declining, while sales at local stores and convenience stores are increasing. No matter what kind of business you do in the future, you will have to consider the most likely buyers: the elderly.
g_text_translate.fr:
La Corée est déjà entrée dans une société vieillissante en 2000 et, d'ici 2030, on s'attend à ce que les personnes âgées représentent plus de 30 % de la population totale du pays. Les personnes âgées sont désormais sous le feu des projecteurs en tant que plus gros acheteurs en Corée. Souvent, les personnes âgées ne dépensent pas trop, car elles sont déjà à la retraite et leur avenir est incertain. Ils ont également une gamme restreinte d’activités, de sorte qu’ils ont tendance à acheter des produits de première nécessité, comme de la nourriture, principalement à la maison. En conséquence, les ventes des grands magasins diminuent, tandis que celles des magasins locaux et des dépanneurs augmentent. Quel que soit le type d’entreprise que vous exercerez à l’avenir, vous devrez considérer les acheteurs les plus probables : les personnes âgées.

#######

They also have a narrow range of activities, so they tend to buy necessities such as food mainly around the house. g_text_translate.es:
Corea ya entró en una sociedad envejecida en el año 2000 y, para 2030, se espera que las personas mayores representen más del 30% de la población total de Corea. Las personas mayores están ahora en el centro de atención como los mayores compradores en Corea. Las personas mayores a menudo no gastan de más porque ya están jubiladas y su futuro es incierto. También tienen una gama limitada de actividades, por lo que tienden a comprar artículos de primera necesidad, como alimentos, principalmente en la casa. Como resultado, las ventas en los grandes almacenes están disminuyendo, mientras que las ventas en las tiendas locales y en las tiendas de conveniencia están aumentando. No importa qué tipo de negocio haga en el futuro, tendrá que considerar a los compradores más probables: las personas mayores.

g_text_translate.id:
Korea telah memasuki masyarakat lansia pada tahun 2000, dan pada tahun 2030, diperkirakan jumlah lansia akan mencapai lebih dari 30% dari total populasi di Korea. Para lansia kini menjadi pusat perhatian sebagai pembeli terbesar di Korea. Para lansia seringkali tidak mengeluarkan uang terlalu banyak karena mereka sudah pensiun dan masa depan mereka tidak pasti. Aktivitas mereka juga terbatas, sehingga mereka cenderung membeli kebutuhan seperti makanan terutama di sekitar rumah. Akibatnya, penjualan di department store menurun, sementara penjualan di toko lokal dan convenience store meningkat. Apa pun jenis bisnis yang Anda lakukan di masa depan, Anda harus mempertimbangkan pembeli yang paling mungkin: orang lanjut usia.

#######

g_text_translate.ja:
韓国は2000年にすでに高齢化社会を迎えており、2030年には韓国の総人口に占める高齢者の割合が30％を超えると予想されています。韓国では高齢者が最大の買い手として注目されている。高齢者はすでに退職しており、将来が不確実であるため、浪費をしないことがよくあります。また、行動範囲も狭いため、食料などの必需品を主に家の周りで購入する傾向があります。その結果、百貨店の売上が減少する一方、地方商店やコンビニエンスストアの売上は増加しています。将来どのようなビジネスを行うにしても、最も可能性の高い買い手である高齢者を考慮する必要があります。

#######

They also have a narrow range of activities, so they tend to buy necessities such as food mainly around the house. g_text_translate.de:
Korea ist bereits im Jahr 2000 in eine alternde Gesellschaft eingetreten, und es wird erwartet, dass ältere Menschen bis 2030 mehr als 30 % der Gesamtbevölkerung in Korea ausmachen werden. Ältere Menschen stehen heute als größte Käufer in Korea im Rampenlicht. Ältere Menschen geben oft nicht zu viel aus, weil sie bereits im Ruhestand sind und ihre Zukunft ungewiss ist. Außerdem haben sie nur ein begrenztes Spektrum an Aktivitäten, weshalb sie dazu neigen, lebensnotwendige Dinge wie Lebensmittel überwiegend rund ums Haus einzukaufen. Infolgedessen gehen die Umsätze in Kaufhäusern zurück, während die Umsätze in lokalen Geschäften und Convenience-Stores steigen. Unabhängig davon, welche Art von Geschäft Sie in Zukunft betreiben, müssen Sie die wahrscheinlichsten Käufer berücksichtigen: ältere Menschen.

g_text_translate.zh-CN:
韩国在2000年就已经进入老龄化社会，预计到2030年，老年人将占韩国总人口的30%以上。如今，老年人作为韩国最大的买家而受到关注。老年人往往不会超支，因为他们已经退休，未来不确定。他们的活动范围也很窄，因此他们倾向于主要在家里购买食物等必需品。因此，百货商店的销售额正在下降，而当地商店和便利店的销售额却在增加。不管你以后做什么样的生意，你都要考虑最有可能的买家：老年人。

Korea has already entered an aged society in 2000, and by 2030, it is expected that the elderly will account for more than 30% of the total population in Korea. The elderly are now in the limelight as the biggest buyers in Korea. Elderly people often do not overspend because they are already retired and their future is uncertain. They also have a narrow range of activities, so they tend to buy necessities such as food mainly around the house. As a result, sales at department stores are declining, while sales at local stores and convenience stores are increasing. No matter what kind of business you do in the future, you will have to consider the most likely buyers: the elderly.
g_text_translate.ru:
Корея уже вступила в общество пожилых людей в 2000 году, и ожидается, что к 2030 году пожилые люди будут составлять более 30% от общей численности населения Кореи. Пожилые люди сейчас находятся в центре внимания как крупнейшие покупатели в Корее. Пожилые люди часто не тратят слишком много, потому что они уже на пенсии и их будущее неопределенно. У них также узкий круг занятий, поэтому они склонны покупать предметы первой необходимости, такие как еда, в основном по дому. В результате продажи в универмагах снижаются, тогда как продажи в местных магазинах и магазинах повседневного спроса растут. Независимо от того, каким бизнесом вы будете заниматься в будущем, вам придется учитывать наиболее вероятных покупателей: пожилых людей.

g_text_translate.th:
เกาหลีได้เข้าสู่สังคมสูงวัยแล้วในปี 2543 และภายในปี 2573 คาดว่าผู้สูงอายุจะมีสัดส่วนมากกว่า 30% ของประชากรทั้งหมดในเกาหลี ปัจจุบันผู้สูงอายุเป็นที่สนใจในฐานะผู้ซื้อรายใหญ่ที่สุดในเกาหลี ผู้สูงอายุมักไม่ใช้จ่ายฟุ่มเฟือยเนื่องจากเกษียณแล้วและอนาคตไม่แน่นอน อีกทั้งยังมีกิจกรรมที่จำกัด จึงมักจะซื้อสิ่งของจำเป็น เช่น อาหาร รอบๆ บ้านเป็นหลัก ส่งผลให้ยอดขายในห้างสรรพสินค้าลดลง ในขณะที่ยอดขายในร้านค้าท้องถิ่นและร้านสะดวกซื้อก็เพิ่มขึ้น ไม่ว่าคุณจะทำธุรกิจประเภทใดในอนาคต คุณจะต้องคำนึงถึงผู้ซื้อที่มีแนวโน้มมากที่สุด นั่นก็คือ ผู้สูงอายุ

#######

Korea has already entered an aged society in 2000, and by 2030, it is expected that the elderly will account for more than 30% of the total population in Korea. The elderly are now in the limelight as the biggest buyers in Korea. Elderly people often do not overspend because they are already retired and their future is uncertain. They also have a narrow range of activities, so they tend to buy necessities such as food mainly around the house. As a result, sales at department stores are declining, while sales at local stores and convenience stores are increasing. No matter what kind of business you do in the future, you will have to consider the most likely buyers: the elderly.
g_text_translate.pt:
A Coreia já entrou numa sociedade envelhecida em 2000 e, em 2030, espera-se que os idosos representem mais de 30% da população total da Coreia. Os idosos estão agora no centro das atenções como os maiores compradores na Coreia. Muitas vezes, os idosos não gastam demasiado porque já estão reformados e o seu futuro é incerto. Eles também têm uma gama restrita de atividades, por isso tendem a comprar bens de primeira necessidade, como alimentos, principalmente em casa. Como resultado, as vendas nas lojas de departamentos estão diminuindo, enquanto as vendas nas lojas locais e de conveniência estão aumentando. Não importa que tipo de negócio você faça no futuro, você terá que considerar os compradores mais prováveis: os idosos.

#######

Korea has already entered an aged society in 2000, and by 2030, it is expected that the elderly will account for more than 30% of the total population in Korea. The elderly are now in the limelight as the biggest buyers in Korea. Elderly people often do not overspend because they are already retired and their future is uncertain. They also have a narrow range of activities, so they tend to buy necessities such as food mainly around the house. As a result, sales at department stores are declining, while sales at local stores and convenience stores are increasing. No matter what kind of business you do in the future, you will have to consider the most likely buyers: the elderly.
g_text_translate.hi:
कोरिया 2000 में ही वृद्ध समाज में प्रवेश कर चुका है, और 2030 तक, यह उम्मीद है कि कोरिया की कुल आबादी में बुजुर्गों की संख्या 30% से अधिक होगी। कोरिया में बुजुर्ग अब सबसे बड़े खरीदार के रूप में सुर्खियों में हैं। बुजुर्ग लोग अक्सर ज़्यादा ख़र्च नहीं करते क्योंकि वे पहले ही सेवानिवृत्त हो चुके होते हैं और उनका भविष्य अनिश्चित होता है। उनकी गतिविधियों की सीमा भी सीमित होती है, इसलिए वे मुख्य रूप से घर के आसपास ही भोजन जैसी ज़रूरत की चीजें खरीदते हैं। परिणामस्वरूप, डिपार्टमेंटल स्टोर्स पर बिक्री घट रही है, जबकि स्थानीय स्टोर्स और सुविधा स्टोरों पर बिक्री बढ़ रही है। इससे कोई फर्क नहीं पड़ता कि आप भविष्य में किस प्रकार का व्यवसाय करते हैं, आपको सबसे संभावित खरीदारों पर विचार करना होगा: बुजुर्ग।

#######

g_text_translate.tr:
Kore 2000 yılında zaten yaşlı bir topluma girmiştir ve 2030 yılına kadar yaşlıların Kore'deki toplam nüfusun %30'undan fazlasını oluşturması beklenmektedir. Artık Kore'deki en büyük alıcılar olarak yaşlılar ilgi odağı. Yaşlı insanlar genellikle emekli oldukları ve gelecekleri belirsiz olduğu için fazla harcama yapmazlar. Ayrıca faaliyet alanları da dar olduğundan yiyecek gibi temel ihtiyaçları evin çevresinden satın alma eğilimindedirler. Sonuç olarak, büyük mağazalardaki satışlar düşerken yerel mağazalardaki ve bakkallardaki satışlar artıyor. Gelecekte ne tür bir iş yapıyor olursanız olun, en muhtemel alıcıları, yani yaşlıları göz önünde bulundurmanız gerekecek.

Chọn suy nghĩ trọng tâm của đoạn văn.
1. Dân số già tăng lên và trở thành vấn đề xã hội.
2. Với sự gia tăng dân số người cao tuổi, ngành công nghiệp nhu yếu phẩm sẽ phát triển.
3. Trong xã hội già hóa, người già sẽ trở thành người tiêu dùng quan trọng.
4. Tương lai của những người già chưa chuẩn bị xong sau khi nghỉ hưu thật bất an.
--------------------

explain.en:
Choose the central thought of the paragraph.
1. The aging population is increasing and becoming a social problem.
2. The increase in the elderly population will develop the daily necessities industry.
3. In an aging society, the elderly will become important consumers in the future.
4. The future of the elderly who are not ready for retirement is uncertain.
--------------------
The elderly are receiving attention as the biggest buyers in Korea. So 3 is the correct answer.
explain.it:

1. L’invecchiamento della popolazione aumenta e diventa un problema sociale.
2. L'aumento della popolazione anziana svilupperà l'industria dei beni di prima necessità.
3. In una società che invecchia, gli anziani diventeranno in futuro importanti consumatori.
4. Il futuro degli anziani che non sono pronti per la pensione è incerto.
--------------------
Gli anziani stanno ricevendo attenzione come i maggiori acquirenti in Corea. Quindi 3 è la risposta corretta.

Pilihlah gagasan utama paragraf tersebut.
1. Populasi menua semakin meningkat dan menjadi masalah sosial.
2. Meningkatnya jumlah penduduk lanjut usia akan mengembangkan industri kebutuhan sehari-hari.
3. Dalam masyarakat yang menua, lansia akan menjadi konsumen penting di masa depan.
4. Masa depan lansia yang belum siap pensiun tidak menentu.
--------------------
Para lansia mendapat perhatian sebagai pembeli terbesar di Korea. Jadi 3 adalah jawaban yang benar.

#######

Choose the central thought of the paragraph.
1. The aging population is increasing and becoming a social problem.
2. The increase in the elderly population will develop the daily necessities industry.
3. In an aging society, the elderly will become important consumers in the future.
4. The future of the elderly who are not ready for retirement is uncertain.
--------------------
The elderly are receiving attention as the biggest buyers in Korea. So 3 is the correct answer.

general.g_text: (        )라는 말이 있다. 높은 곳에 이르기 위해서는 낮은 곳부터 차근차근 밟아야 하듯이 일의 순서를 생각하지 않으면 목표한 것을 얻기가 쉽지 않은 법이다. 그 어떤 위대한 사람도 단번에 높은 곳으로 뛰어오른 적은 없다. 공부도 마찬가지다. 기본 원리를 익히지 않고서 문제부터 해결하려는 조급한 마음을 버려야 한다. 기초부터 탄탄히 다져야만 문제 앞에서 당당해질 수 있다.

g_text_translate.vi: Có câu nói rằng (        ). Cũng giống như bạn phải bước từng bước từ nơi thấp để đến nơi cao, bạn sẽ không dễ dàng đạt được mục tiêu nếu bạn không suy nghĩ về thứ tự của mọi việc. Cũng không một vĩ nhân nào một lần có thể nhảy lên vị trí cao cả. Trong học tập cũng vậy. Chúng ta phải từ bỏ sự thiếu kiên nhẫn không học các nguyên tắc cơ bản mà lại muốn giải quyết câu hỏi trước. Bạn có thể tự tin khi đối mặt với câu hỏi chỉ bằng cách củng cố những kiến ​​thức cơ bản.
g_text_translate.en: There is a saying that a journey of a thousand miles starts with one step. In order to reach a high place, it is not easy to achieve the goal if you do not think about the order of work. No great man has ever jumped to great heights at once. Study is the same. We must throw away the impatient mind of trying to solve the problem first without learning the basic principles. Only when the foundation is firmly established can one be confident in front of problems.
g_text_translate.it:
Si dice che un viaggio di mille miglia inizia con un passo. Per raggiungere una posizione elevata, non è facile raggiungere l'obiettivo se non si pensa all'ordine di lavoro. Nessun grande uomo è mai riuscito a raggiungere grandi altezze in una sola volta. Lo studio è lo stesso. Dobbiamo buttare via la mente impaziente di provare prima a risolvere il problema senza apprendere i principi di base. Solo quando le fondamenta sono saldamente stabilite si può essere fiduciosi di fronte ai problemi.

#######

There is a saying that a journey of a thousand miles starts with one step. In order to reach a high place, it is not easy to achieve the goal if you do not think about the order of work. No great man has ever jumped to great heights at once. Study is the same. We must throw away the impatient mind of trying to solve the problem first without learning the basic principles. Only when the foundation is firmly established can one be confident in front of problems.
g_text_translate.fr:
Il y a un dicton qui dit qu'un voyage de mille kilomètres commence par un premier pas. Pour atteindre un haut niveau, il n’est pas facile d’atteindre l’objectif si l’on ne pense pas à l’ordre des travaux. Aucun grand homme n’a jamais sauté très haut d’un coup. L’étude est la même. Nous devons nous débarrasser de l’impatience qui consiste à tenter d’abord de résoudre le problème sans en apprendre les principes de base. Ce n’est que lorsque les fondations sont solidement établies que l’on peut être confiant face aux problèmes.

#######

Only when the foundation is firmly established can one be confident in front of problems.
g_text_translate.es:
Hay un dicho que dice que un viaje de mil millas comienza con un paso. Para alcanzar un lugar alto, no es fácil lograr la meta si no se piensa en el orden del trabajo. Ningún gran hombre ha saltado jamás a grandes alturas a la vez. El estudio es el mismo. Debemos desechar la mente impaciente de intentar resolver el problema primero sin aprender los principios básicos. Sólo cuando las bases están firmemente establecidas se puede tener confianza ante los problemas.

g_text_translate.id:
Ada pepatah yang mengatakan bahwa perjalanan seribu mil dimulai dengan satu langkah. Untuk mencapai tempat yang tinggi, tidak mudah mencapai tujuan jika tidak memikirkan urutan pekerjaannya. Tidak ada orang hebat yang pernah melompat ke ketinggian sekaligus. Belajar pun sama. Kita harus membuang pikiran tidak sabar yang berusaha menyelesaikan masalah terlebih dahulu tanpa mempelajari prinsip dasarnya. Hanya ketika fondasinya kokoh, barulah seseorang bisa percaya diri dalam menghadapi masalah.

g_text_translate.ja:
千里の道も一歩からという諺があります。高いところへ行くためには、仕事の順序を考えないと目標を達成するのは簡単ではありません。偉大な人物で一度に高みへジャンプした人はいません。勉強も同じです。基本原理を学ばずにまず問題を解決しようとする焦りは捨てなければなりません。基礎がしっかりと確立されて初めて、問題の前でも自信を持つことができます。

g_text_translate.de:
Es gibt ein Sprichwort, dass eine Reise von tausend Meilen mit einem Schritt beginnt. Um eine hohe Position zu erreichen, ist es nicht einfach, das Ziel zu erreichen, wenn man nicht über die Reihenfolge der Arbeit nachdenkt. Kein großer Mann ist jemals auf einmal zu großen Höhen gesprungen. Das Studium ist das Gleiche. Wir müssen die Ungeduld aufgeben, zuerst zu versuchen, das Problem zu lösen, ohne die Grundprinzipien zu erlernen. Nur wenn das Fundament gefestigt ist, kann man Problemen mit Zuversicht begegnen.

एक कहावत है कि हजारों मील की यात्रा एक कदम से शुरू होती है। किसी ऊंचे स्थान पर पहुंचने के लिए यदि आप कार्य के क्रम के बारे में नहीं सोचेंगे तो लक्ष्य हासिल करना आसान नहीं है। कोई भी महान व्यक्ति एक बार में महान ऊंचाइयों तक नहीं पहुंचा है। पढ़ाई भी वैसी ही है. हमें बुनियादी सिद्धांतों को सीखे बिना पहले समस्या को हल करने की कोशिश करने के अधीर मन को त्याग देना चाहिए। जब नींव मजबूती से स्थापित हो तभी कोई समस्याओं के सामने आश्वस्त हो सकता है।

#######

There is a saying that a journey of a thousand miles starts with one step. In order to reach a high place, it is not easy to achieve the goal if you do not think about the order of work. No great man has ever jumped to great heights at once. Study is the same. We must throw away the impatient mind of trying to solve the problem first without learning the basic principles. Only when the foundation is firmly established can one be confident in front of problems.
g_text_translate.tr:
Bin kilometrelik yolculuk bir adımla başlar diye bir söz vardır. Yüksek bir yere ulaşmak için iş sırasını düşünmezseniz hedefe ulaşmak kolay değildir. Hiçbir büyük adam aynı anda çok yükseklere atlamadı. Çalışma da aynı. Temel ilkeleri öğrenmeden önce sorunu çözmeye çalışan sabırsız zihniyeti bir kenara bırakmalıyız. Ancak temel sağlam bir şekilde kurulduğunda kişi sorunlar karşısında kendinden emin olabilir.

explain.vi:
Chọn suy nghĩ trọng tâm của đoạn văn.
1. Phải từ bỏ sự nóng vội thì mới có thể giải quyết câu hỏi một cách nhanh chóng.
2. Phải suy nghĩ về thứ tự công việc trước rồi mới làm việc.
3. Phải học từng bước từ nguyên tắc cơ bản.
4. Nếu muốn nhận được thành tích tốt thì phải giải nhiều câu hỏi.
--------------------
Đoạn văn kết luận rằng ta có thể tự tin đối mặt với câu hỏi chỉ bằng cách củng cố những kiến ​​thức cơ bản, tức là phải học từng bước từ nguyên tắc cơ bản. 3 là đáp án đúng.
explain.en:

2. You must think of the order of work first and then work.

4. You have to solve a lot of problems to get good grades.

The paragraph concludes that one can confidently face problems only by learning the basic principles. 3 is the correct answer.

4. You have to solve a lot of problems to get good grades.
--------------------

explain.th:

1. คุณสามารถแก้ไขปัญหาได้อย่างรวดเร็วเฉพาะเมื่อคุณละทิ้งจิตใจที่ใจร้อนเท่านั้น

explain.hi:
पैराग्राफ का केंद्रीय विचार चुनें.
1. आप समस्याओं का शीघ्र समाधान तभी कर सकते हैं, जब आप अपने अधीर मन को दूर फेंक देंगे।
2. आपको पहले काम के क्रम के बारे में सोचना चाहिए और फिर काम करना चाहिए।

4. अच्छे ग्रेड पाने के लिए आपको कई समस्याओं का समाधान करना होगा।
------------------------

general.g_text: 최근 우리 사회에 웰빙 바람이 한창이다. 하지만 웰빙 바람이 대부분 음식 섭취와 주거 등에 한정된 육체적인 건강 운동으로 받아들여지고 있는 점은 우려를 낳게 한다. 정신적인 평화와 서로에 대해 감사하는 마음, 이것을 보통의 삶으로 일상화시키는 변화 없이는 참된 웰빙은 어렵다. 경쟁이 일상화된 환경에서 (        ) 식의 살벌한 조직 생활을 하면서 유기농 야채를 먹고 건강한 집에서 산다고 해서 웰빙이 될 리 없다.

g_text_translate.vi: Gần đây đang là đỉnh điểm của làn sóng well-being trong xã hội của chúng ta. Tuy nhiên, điều đáng lo ngại là làn sóng well-being phần lớn được chấp nhận như một phong trào sức khỏe mang tính thể chất bị giới hạn trong việc ăn uống và cư trú. Well-being thật sự sẽ khó đạt được nếu không có sự bình yên về tinh thần, sự trân trọng lẫn nhau và những thay đổi khiến điều này trở thành một phần bình thường của cuộc sống. Trong một môi trường cạnh tranh trở nên phổ biến, vừa sống đời sống tổ chức tàn bạo theo kiểu (        ) vừa ăn rau hữu cơ và sống trong một ngôi nhà khỏe mạnh không có lý gì lại là well-being.
g_text_translate.en: Recently, the well-being wind is in full swing in our society. However, the fact that the well-being wind is mostly accepted as a physical health movement limited to food intake and housing raises concerns. True well-being is difficult without peace of mind, a sense of appreciation for each other, and the changes that make this a normal part of life. Eating organic vegetables and living in a healthy house while living in a bloody organized life of do-or-die in an environment where competition has become commonplace does not mean well-being.
g_text_translate.it:
Recentemente, il vento del benessere è in pieno svolgimento nella nostra società. Tuttavia, il fatto che il vento del benessere sia per lo più accettato come un movimento per la salute fisica limitato all’assunzione di cibo e all’abitazione solleva preoccupazioni. Il vero benessere è difficile senza la tranquillità, un senso di apprezzamento reciproco e i cambiamenti che rendono tutto questo una parte normale della vita. Mangiare verdure biologiche e vivere in una casa sana vivendo una vita maledettamente organizzata fatta di "fai o muori" in un ambiente in cui la competizione è diventata un luogo comune non significa benessere.

#######

Recently, the well-being wind is in full swing in our society. However, the fact that the well-being wind is mostly accepted as a physical health movement limited to food intake and housing raises concerns. True well-being is difficult without peace of mind, a sense of appreciation for each other, and the changes that make this a normal part of life. Eating organic vegetables and living in a healthy house while living in a bloody organized life of do-or-die in an environment where competition has become commonplace does not mean well-being.
g_text_translate.fr:
Depuis peu, le vent du bien-être bat son plein dans notre société. Cependant, le fait que le vent du bien-être soit majoritairement accepté comme un mouvement de santé physique limité à l’alimentation et au logement suscite des inquiétudes. Le véritable bien-être est difficile sans la tranquillité d’esprit, sans un sentiment d’appréciation mutuelle et sans les changements qui en font une partie normale de la vie. Manger des légumes biologiques et vivre dans une maison saine tout en vivant une vie sanglante et organisée, dans un environnement où la compétition est devenue monnaie courante, ne signifie pas bien-être.

#######

Recently, the well-being wind is in full swing in our society. However, the fact that the well-being wind is mostly accepted as a physical health movement limited to food intake and housing raises concerns. True well-being is difficult without peace of mind, a sense of appreciation for each other, and the changes that make this a normal part of life. Eating organic vegetables and living in a healthy house while living in a bloody organized life of do-or-die in an environment where competition has become commonplace does not mean well-being.
g_text_translate.es:
Recientemente, el viento de bienestar está soplando en nuestra sociedad. Sin embargo, el hecho de que el viento del bienestar se acepte mayoritariamente como un movimiento de salud física limitado a la ingesta de alimentos y la vivienda genera preocupación. El verdadero bienestar es difícil sin tranquilidad, un sentido de aprecio mutuo y los cambios que hacen de esto una parte normal de la vida. Comer vegetales orgánicos y vivir en una casa saludable mientras se vive una vida sangrienta y organizada de vida o muerte en un ambiente donde la competencia se ha vuelto algo común no significa bienestar.

#######

Recently, the well-being wind is in full swing in our society. However, the fact that the well-being wind is mostly accepted as a physical health movement limited to food intake and housing raises concerns. True well-being is difficult without peace of mind, a sense of appreciation for each other, and the changes that make this a normal part of life. g_text_translate.id:
Akhir-akhir ini, angin kesejahteraan sedang bertiup kencang di masyarakat kita. Namun, fakta bahwa angin kesejahteraan sebagian besar diterima sebagai gerakan kesehatan fisik yang terbatas pada asupan makanan dan perumahan menimbulkan kekhawatiran. Kesejahteraan sejati sulit dicapai tanpa ketenangan pikiran, rasa penghargaan satu sama lain, dan perubahan yang menjadikan hal ini sebagai bagian normal dalam hidup. Mengonsumsi sayuran organik dan hidup di rumah yang sehat sambil menjalani kehidupan yang terorganisir dan penuh darah, hidup atau mati di lingkungan di mana persaingan sudah menjadi hal yang biasa tidak berarti kesejahteraan.

#######

Recently, the well-being wind is in full swing in our society. However, the fact that the well-being wind is mostly accepted as a physical health movement limited to food intake and housing raises concerns. True well-being is difficult without peace of mind, a sense of appreciation for each other, and the changes that make this a normal part of life. Eating organic vegetables and living in a healthy house while living in a bloody organized life of do-or-die in an environment where competition has become commonplace does not mean well-being.
g_text_translate.ja:
昨今、私たちの社会にはウェルビーイングの風が吹き荒れています。しかし、ウェルビーイングの風が、食事と住居に限定された身体的健康運動として主に受け入れられているという事実は、懸念を引き起こします。心の平安、お互いへの感謝の気持ち、そしてそれを日常の一部にする変化がなければ、真の幸福は困難です。競争が当たり前になった環境で、やるか死ぬかという血みどろの組織的な生活を送りながら、有機野菜を食べ、健康的な家に住むことが幸福を意味するわけではありません。

#######

However, the fact that the well-being wind is mostly accepted as a physical health movement limited to food intake and housing raises concerns. True well-being is difficult without peace of mind, a sense of appreciation for each other, and the changes that make this a normal part of life. g_text_translate.de:
Seit Kurzem ist in unserer Gesellschaft der Wohlfühlwind in vollem Gange. Allerdings gibt die Tatsache, dass der Wohlfühlwind größtenteils als Bewegung für körperliche Gesundheit akzeptiert wird, die sich auf Nahrungsaufnahme und Wohnen beschränkt, Anlass zur Sorge. Wahres Wohlbefinden ist ohne Seelenfrieden, ein Gefühl der Wertschätzung füreinander und die Veränderungen, die dies zu einem normalen Teil des Lebens machen, schwierig. Bio-Gemüse zu essen und in einem gesunden Zuhause zu leben, während man in einem Umfeld, in dem Konkurrenz alltäglich geworden ist, ein verdammt organisiertes Leben auf Leben und Tod führt, bedeutet nicht, dass man sich wohlfühlt.

True well-being is difficult without peace of mind, a sense of appreciation for each other, and the changes that make this a normal part of life. Eating organic vegetables and living in a healthy house while living in a bloody organized life of do-or-die in an environment where competition has become commonplace does not mean well-being.
g_text_translate.zh-CN:
最近，社会上刮起了一股幸福之风。然而，健康风大多被认为是一种仅限于食物摄入和住房的身体健康运动，这一事实引起了人们的担忧。如果没有内心的平静、彼此的欣赏感以及使这成为生活正常一部分的改变，真正的幸福是很难实现的。吃有机蔬菜，住在健康的房子里，在竞争已经司空见惯的环境中过着生死攸关的血腥组织生活，并不意味着幸福。

g_text_translate.zh-TW:
最近，社會上刮起了一股幸福之風。然而，健康風大多被認為是一種僅限於食物攝入和住房的身體健康運動，這一事實引起了人們的擔憂。如果沒有內心的平靜、彼此的欣賞感以及使這成為生活正常一部分的改變，真正的幸福是很難實現的。吃有機蔬菜，住在健康的房子裡，在競爭已經司空見慣的環境中過著生死攸關的血腥組織生活，並不意味著幸福。

#######

True well-being is difficult without peace of mind, a sense of appreciation for each other, and the changes that make this a normal part of life. g_text_translate.ru:
В последнее время в нашем обществе полным ходом идет ветер благополучия. Однако тот факт, что ветер благополучия в основном воспринимается как движение за физическое здоровье, ограничивающееся приемом пищи и жильем, вызывает обеспокоенность. Настоящее благополучие невозможно без душевного спокойствия, чувства признательности друг к другу и тех изменений, которые делают это нормальной частью жизни. Употребление в пищу органических овощей и жизнь в здоровом доме, одновременно живя в кровавой организованной жизни по принципу «сделай или умри» в среде, где конкуренция стала обычным явлением, не означает благополучие.

g_text_translate.th:
ช่วงนี้กระแสความอยู่ดีมีสุขกำลังมาแรงในสังคมของเรา อย่างไรก็ตาม ความจริงที่ว่าลมความเป็นอยู่ที่ดีส่วนใหญ่ได้รับการยอมรับว่าเป็นการเคลื่อนไหวด้านสุขภาพกายที่จำกัดแค่การบริโภคอาหารและที่อยู่อาศัย ทำให้เกิดข้อกังวล ความเป็นอยู่ที่ดีที่แท้จริงนั้นเป็นเรื่องยากหากไม่มีความสงบจิตใจ ความรู้สึกขอบคุณต่อกัน และการเปลี่ยนแปลงที่ทำให้สิ่งนี้เป็นเรื่องปกติของชีวิต การรับประทานผักออร์แกนิกและการใช้ชีวิตในบ้านที่มีสุขภาพดีในขณะที่ใช้ชีวิตแบบนองเลือดที่ต้องทำหรือตายในสภาพแวดล้อมที่การแข่งขันกลายเป็นเรื่องปกติไม่ได้หมายความว่าจะมีความเป็นอยู่ที่ดี

#######

However, the fact that the well-being wind is mostly accepted as a physical health movement limited to food intake and housing raises concerns. True well-being is difficult without peace of mind, a sense of appreciation for each other, and the changes that make this a normal part of life. Eating organic vegetables and living in a healthy house while living in a bloody organized life of do-or-die in an environment where competition has become commonplace does not mean well-being.
g_text_translate.pt:
Recentemente, o vento do bem-estar está a todo vapor em nossa sociedade. No entanto, o facto de o vento do bem-estar ser maioritariamente aceite como um movimento de saúde física limitado à ingestão de alimentos e à habitação suscita preocupações. O verdadeiro bem-estar é difícil sem paz de espírito, um sentimento de apreço mútuo e as mudanças que tornam isso uma parte normal da vida. Comer vegetais orgânicos e viver numa casa saudável enquanto se vive uma vida sangrenta e organizada de vida ou morte num ambiente onde a competição se tornou comum não significa bem-estar.

g_text_translate.hi:
हाल ही में, हमारे समाज में कल्याणकारी बयार जोरों पर है। हालाँकि, यह तथ्य कि कल्याणकारी हवा को ज्यादातर भोजन सेवन और आवास तक सीमित शारीरिक स्वास्थ्य आंदोलन के रूप में स्वीकार किया जाता है, चिंता पैदा करता है। मन की शांति, एक-दूसरे के लिए सराहना की भावना और उन बदलावों के बिना सच्ची भलाई मुश्किल है जो इसे जीवन का एक सामान्य हिस्सा बनाते हैं। ऐसे माहौल में जहां प्रतिस्पर्धा आम हो गई है, करो या मरो के खूनी संगठित जीवन में रहते हुए जैविक सब्जियां खाना और एक स्वस्थ घर में रहना, कल्याण का मतलब नहीं है।

g_text_translate.tr:
Son zamanlarda toplumumuzda refah rüzgarı tüm hızıyla esiyor. Ancak refah rüzgarının çoğunlukla gıda alımı ve barınmayla sınırlı bir fiziksel sağlık hareketi olarak kabul edilmesi endişeleri artırıyor. Gerçek refah, iç huzuru, birbirini takdir etme duygusu ve bunu yaşamın normal bir parçası haline getiren değişiklikler olmadan zordur. Rekabetin sıradanlaştığı bir ortamda kanlı, organize bir yap ya da öl hayatı yaşarken, organik sebze yemek ve sağlıklı bir evde yaşamak, refah anlamına gelmiyor.

Điền cụm từ thích hợp vào chỗ trống (   ).
1. Bắt gà ăn xong rồi lại chìa chân vịt ra (giấu đầu lòi đuôi)
2. Bơi chạm đất (dễ như trở bàn tay)
3. Sống hoặc bất tỉnh (sống còn)
4. Ăn chim trĩ và ăn luôn cả trứng (một công hai việc)
--------------------

explain.vi:
Chọn suy nghĩ trọng tâm của đoạn văn.
1. Đời sống tổ chức và well-being là mối quan hệ không thể cùng tồn tại.
2. Làn sóng well-being đang thổi vào lĩnh vực thực phẩm và nhà ở. 
3. Well-being đề cập đến sức khỏe thể chất.
4. Một cuộc sống khỏe mạnh về mặt tinh thần là well-being thực sự.
--------------------
Well-being thật sự sẽ khó đạt được nếu không có sự bình yên về tinh thần. Như vậy 4 là đáp án đúng.
explain.en:
Choose the central thought of the paragraph.
1. Organized life and well-being are incompatible.
2. The wind of well-being is blowing in the food and housing sectors.
3. Well-being refers to physical health.
4. A mentally healthy life is true well-being.
--------------------
True well-being would be difficult to achieve without peace of mind. So 4 is the correct answer.

4. Una vita mentalmente sana è il vero benessere.
--------------------
Il vero benessere sarebbe difficile da raggiungere senza la pace della mente. Quindi 4 è la risposta corretta.

general.g_text: 세상에서 가장 힘이 세고 무서운 동물은 무엇일까? 아마도 그것은 사자나 코끼리처럼 몸집이 큰 동물일 것이다. 그렇다면 이러한 동물들도 무서워하는 것이 있을까? 그렇다. 세상에서 가장 힘이 세고 무서운 동물도 무서워하는 것이 있게 마련이다. 예를 들면, 사자는 모기를 무서워한다. 동물의 왕인 사자가 모기를 무서워한다니 (       ) 일이다. 또 코끼리는 피를 빨아 먹는 거머리를 무서워하고 하늘을 지배하는 독수리는 거미를 무서워한다. 이것은 몸집이 크고 힘이 강해서 세상에 두려울 것이 없어 보이는 것이라도 무서워하는 것이 있고 몸집이 작고 약한 것이라도 다른 동물에게 두려움을 줄 수 있다는 말이다.

g_text_translate.vi: Động vật nào mạnh nhất và đáng sợ nhất trên thế giới? Có lẽ là những loài động vật có thân hình to lớn như sư tử và voi. Nếu vậy thì những động vật này cũng có thứ mà chúng sợ hãi chứ? Đúng vậy. Đương nhiên có những thứ mà động vật mạnh nhất và đáng sợ nhất trên thế giới cũng sợ hãi. Ví dụ, sư tử sợ muỗi. Sư tử, vua của muôn loài, lại sợ muỗi à, một việc (        ) đấy. Ngoài ra, voi sợ con đỉa hút máu và đại bàng, loài thống lĩnh bầu trời, sợ nhện. Điều này có nghĩa là ngay cả khi động vật trông như thể không có gì phải sợ trên thế giới bởi vì kích thước to lớn và sức mạnh của chúng, thì vẫn có những thứ mà chúng sợ hãi, và ngay cả khi chúng nhỏ bé và yếu ớt, chúng cũng có thể gây ra sự sợ hãi cho các loài động vật khác.
g_text_translate.en: What is the strongest and most feared animal in the world? Perhaps it is a large animal such as a lion or an elephant. So what are these animals afraid of? Yes. Even the strongest and most fearsome animal in the world has something to fear. For example, lions are afraid of mosquitoes. It is unknown that the lion, the king of animals, is afraid of mosquitoes. Also, elephants are afraid of blood-sucking leeches, and eagles who rule the sky are afraid of spiders. This means that even if animals look as if there is nothing to fear in the world because they are large and strong, there are things that scare them, and even small and weak animals can give fear to other animals.
g_text_translate.it:
Qual è l'animale più forte e temuto del mondo? Forse è un grande animale come un leone o un elefante. Allora di cosa hanno paura questi animali? SÌ. Anche l'animale più forte e temibile del mondo ha qualcosa da temere. Ad esempio, i leoni hanno paura delle zanzare. Non è noto che il leone, il re degli animali, abbia paura delle zanzare. Inoltre, gli elefanti hanno paura delle sanguisughe succhiasangue e le aquile che governano il cielo hanno paura dei ragni. Ciò significa che anche se gli animali sembrano non avere nulla da temere al mondo perché sono grandi e forti, ci sono cose che li spaventano, e anche gli animali piccoli e deboli possono spaventare gli altri animali.

#######

What is the strongest and most feared animal in the world? Perhaps it is a large animal such as a lion or an elephant. So what are these animals afraid of? Yes. Even the strongest and most fearsome animal in the world has something to fear. For example, lions are afraid of mosquitoes. It is unknown that the lion, the king of animals, is afraid of mosquitoes. Also, elephants are afraid of blood-sucking leeches, and eagles who rule the sky are afraid of spiders. This means that even if animals look as if there is nothing to fear in the world because they are large and strong, there are things that scare them, and even small and weak animals can give fear to other animals.
g_text_translate.fr:
Quel est l'animal le plus fort et le plus redouté au monde ? Il s’agit peut-être d’un gros animal comme un lion ou un éléphant. Alors de quoi ont peur ces animaux ? Oui. Même l’animal le plus fort et le plus redoutable du monde a quelque chose à craindre. Par exemple, les lions ont peur des moustiques. On ignore que le lion, roi des animaux, a peur des moustiques. De plus, les éléphants ont peur des sangsues suceuses de sang et les aigles qui gouvernent le ciel ont peur des araignées. Cela signifie que même si les animaux semblent n'avoir rien à craindre au monde parce qu'ils sont grands et forts, il y a des choses qui leur font peur, et même des animaux petits et faibles peuvent faire peur aux autres animaux.

#######

What is the strongest and most feared animal in the world? Perhaps it is a large animal such as a lion or an elephant. So what are these animals afraid of? Yes. Even the strongest and most fearsome animal in the world has something to fear. For example, lions are afraid of mosquitoes. It is unknown that the lion, the king of animals, is afraid of mosquitoes. Also, elephants are afraid of blood-sucking leeches, and eagles who rule the sky are afraid of spiders. This means that even if animals look as if there is nothing to fear in the world because they are large and strong, there are things that scare them, and even small and weak animals can give fear to other animals.
g_text_translate.es:
¿Cuál es el animal más fuerte y temido del mundo? Quizás sea un animal grande como un león o un elefante. Entonces, ¿a qué le temen estos animales? Sí. Incluso el animal más fuerte y temible del mundo tiene algo que temer. Por ejemplo, los leones temen a los mosquitos. Se desconoce que el león, rey de los animales, le tiene miedo a los mosquitos. Además, los elefantes temen a las sanguijuelas chupadoras de sangre y las águilas que gobiernan el cielo temen a las arañas. Esto significa que incluso si los animales parecen no tener nada que temer en el mundo porque son grandes y fuertes, hay cosas que los asustan, e incluso los animales pequeños y débiles pueden dar miedo a otros animales.

#######

What is the strongest and most feared animal in the world? Perhaps it is a large animal such as a lion or an elephant. So what are these animals afraid of? Yes. Also, elephants are afraid of blood-sucking leeches, and eagles who rule the sky are afraid of spiders. This means that even if animals look as if there is nothing to fear in the world because they are large and strong, there are things that scare them, and even small and weak animals can give fear to other animals.
g_text_translate.id:
Hewan apa yang paling kuat dan paling ditakuti di dunia? Mungkin binatang besar seperti singa atau gajah. Jadi apa yang ditakuti hewan-hewan ini? Ya. Bahkan hewan terkuat dan paling menakutkan di dunia pun memiliki sesuatu yang perlu ditakuti. Misalnya saja singa yang takut terhadap nyamuk. Tidak diketahui apakah singa, raja binatang, takut terhadap nyamuk. Selain itu, gajah takut pada lintah penghisap darah, dan elang yang menguasai langit takut pada laba-laba. Artinya meskipun hewan terlihat seolah-olah tidak ada yang perlu ditakuti di dunia ini karena mereka besar dan kuat, ada hal-hal yang membuat mereka takut, dan bahkan hewan kecil dan lemah pun dapat memberikan rasa takut kepada hewan lain.

g_text_translate.ja:
世界で最も強く、最も恐れられている動物は何ですか?おそらくライオンやゾウなどの大きな動物でしょう。それでは、これらの動物は何を恐れているのでしょうか？はい。世界で最も強くて最も恐ろしい動物にも、恐れるものがあります。たとえば、ライオンは蚊を恐れます。百獣の王ライオンが蚊を恐れていることは知られていません。また、ゾウは吸血ヒルを恐れ、空を支配するワシはクモを恐れます。これは、大きくて強い動物であっても、世の中には何も怖くないように見えても、実は怖いものがあるし、小さくて弱い動物であっても、他の動物に恐怖を与えることがあるということです。

g_text_translate.de:
Welches ist das stärkste und am meisten gefürchtete Tier der Welt? Vielleicht ist es ein großes Tier wie ein Löwe oder ein Elefant. Wovor haben diese Tiere also Angst? Ja. Selbst das stärkste und furchterregendste Tier der Welt hat etwas zu fürchten. Löwen haben zum Beispiel Angst vor Mücken. Es ist nicht bekannt, dass der Löwe, der König der Tiere, Angst vor Mücken hat. Außerdem haben Elefanten Angst vor blutsaugenden Blutegeln und Adler, die den Himmel beherrschen, haben Angst vor Spinnen. Das heißt, auch wenn Tiere so aussehen, als gäbe es auf der Welt nichts zu fürchten, weil sie groß und stark sind, gibt es Dinge, die ihnen Angst machen, und selbst kleine und schwache Tiere können anderen Tieren Angst machen.

g_text_translate.zh-CN:
世界上最强壮、最可怕的动物是什么？也许是狮子或大象等大型动物。那么这些动物害怕什么呢？是的。即使是世界上最强大、最可怕的动物也有令人恐惧的东西。例如，狮子害怕蚊子。殊不知，动物之王狮子竟然害怕蚊子。还有，大象害怕吸血的水蛭，统治天空的老鹰害怕蜘蛛。这意味着，即使动物看起来世界上没有什么好害怕的，因为它们又大又强壮，但还是有一些东西让它们害怕，甚至弱小的动物也会让其他动物感到恐惧。

g_text_translate.ru:
Какое самое сильное и самое страшное животное в мире? Возможно, это крупное животное, например, лев или слон. Так чего же боятся эти животные? Да. Даже самому сильному и грозному животному в мире есть чего бояться. Например, львы боятся комаров. Неизвестно, боится ли лев, царь зверей, комаров. Также слоны боятся кровососущих пиявок, а орлы, владеющие небом, боятся пауков. Это означает, что даже если животные выглядят так, будто в мире нечего бояться, потому что они большие и сильные, есть вещи, которые их пугают, и даже маленькие и слабые животные могут наводить страх на других животных.

g_text_translate.th:
สัตว์ที่แข็งแกร่งและน่ากลัวที่สุดในโลกคืออะไร? บางทีอาจเป็นสัตว์ขนาดใหญ่เช่นสิงโตหรือช้าง แล้วสัตว์พวกนี้กลัวอะไรล่ะ? ใช่. แม้แต่สัตว์ที่แข็งแกร่งและน่ากลัวที่สุดในโลกก็ยังมีสิ่งที่ต้องกลัว เช่น สิงโตกลัวยุง ไม่ทราบว่าสิงโตเป็นราชาแห่งสัตว์ต่างๆ กลัวยุง นอกจากนี้ ช้างยังกลัวปลิงดูดเลือด และนกอินทรีที่ครองท้องฟ้าก็กลัวแมงมุม ซึ่งหมายความว่าแม้ว่าสัตว์จะดูราวกับว่าไม่มีอะไรต้องกลัวในโลกนี้เนื่องจากพวกมันตัวใหญ่และแข็งแรง แต่ก็มีสิ่งที่ทำให้พวกเขากลัว และแม้แต่สัตว์ตัวเล็กและอ่อนแอก็สามารถสร้างความหวาดกลัวให้กับสัตว์อื่น ๆ ได้

g_text_translate.pt:
Qual é o animal mais forte e temido do mundo? Talvez seja um animal grande, como um leão ou um elefante. Então, do que esses animais têm medo? Sim. Até o animal mais forte e temível do mundo tem algo a temer. Por exemplo, os leões têm medo de mosquitos. Não se sabe que o leão, o rei dos animais, tem medo de mosquitos. Além disso, os elefantes têm medo de sanguessugas sugadoras de sangue, e as águias que governam o céu têm medo de aranhas. Isto significa que mesmo que os animais pareçam não ter nada a temer no mundo porque são grandes e fortes, há coisas que os assustam, e mesmo os animais pequenos e fracos podem causar medo a outros animais.

g_text_translate.hi:
दुनिया का सबसे ताकतवर और सबसे डरावना जानवर कौन सा है? शायद यह शेर या हाथी जैसा कोई बड़ा जानवर है। तो ये जानवर किससे डरते हैं? हाँ। यहां तक ​​कि दुनिया के सबसे ताकतवर और सबसे डरावने जानवर से भी डरने की जरूरत है। उदाहरण के लिए, शेर मच्छरों से डरते हैं। यह अज्ञात है कि जानवरों का राजा शेर, मच्छरों से डरता है। इसके अलावा, हाथी खून चूसने वाली जोंकों से डरते हैं, और आकाश पर राज करने वाले बाज मकड़ियों से डरते हैं। इसका मतलब यह है कि भले ही जानवर ऐसे दिखते हैं मानो दुनिया में डरने की कोई बात नहीं है क्योंकि वे बड़े और मजबूत हैं, फिर भी ऐसी चीजें हैं जो उन्हें डराती हैं, और यहां तक ​​​​कि छोटे और कमजोर जानवर भी अन्य जानवरों को डर दे सकते हैं।

g_text_translate.tr:
Dünyanın en güçlü ve en korkulan hayvanı hangisidir? Belki aslan ya da fil gibi büyük bir hayvandır. Peki bu hayvanlar neden korkuyor? Evet. Dünyadaki en güçlü ve en korkunç hayvanın bile korkacak bir şeyi vardır. Mesela aslanlar sivrisineklerden korkar. Hayvanların kralı aslanın sivrisineklerden korktuğu bilinmiyor. Ayrıca filler kan emen sülüklerden, gökyüzüne hükmeden kartallar ise örümceklerden korkar. Bu şu anlama gelir: Hayvanlar büyük ve güçlü oldukları için dünyada korkacak hiçbir şey yokmuş gibi görünseler bile onları korkutan şeyler vardır ve küçük ve zayıf hayvanlar bile diğer hayvanlara korku verebilir.

Điền cụm từ thích hợp vào chỗ trống (   ).
1. Va vào tường (gặp chướng ngại hay sự khó khăn)
2. Biết rồi lại không biết (việc nào đó không hiểu rõ, không biết trước được)
3. Đổ mồ hôi tay (trong lòng rất căng thẳng do tình huống cấp bách)
4. Đeo kính màu để nhìn (mang theo định kiến nào đó và đánh giá người khác)
--------------------
Việc sư tử lại sợ muỗi là một việc có thể người đọc không biết rõ. Do đó 2 là đáp án đúng.
explain.en:
Choose the right one to put in (   ).
1. Hit the wall
2. We never know
3. Be at the edge of your seat
4. Look on something with prejudiced eyes
--------------------
The fact that lions are afraid of mosquitoes is something that readers may not know. Therefore 2 is the correct answer.

general.g_text: 늘 젊은이들로 북적이는 대림미술관은 미술품 전시회보다 (        ) 부대 행사가 더 인기 있는 미술관이다. 홍대 앞 파티 문화를 전시장에 도입했고, 밴드 공연도 하고, DJ가 랩을 선보이기도 한다. 대림미술관이 지향하는 목표는 명료하다. 근엄한 얼굴을 벗고 '재미있는 미술관'을 만드는 것이다. 사진 촬영에 제한을 두는 기존 미술관과는 달리 마음껏 사진도 찍을 수 있어 사람들은 미술관이 더 이상 멀게 느껴지지 않아서 좋다고 말한다. 대림미술관은 앞으로 미술관이 지향해야 할 방향을 보여 주고 있는 것이다.

g_text_translate.vi: Bảo tàng mỹ thuật Daelim, nơi luôn đông đúc giới trẻ, là một bảo tàng mỹ thuật có các sự kiện đi kèm (        ) được yêu thích hơn cả các triển lãm mỹ thuật. Văn hóa tiệc tùng trước Hongdae đã được áp dụng ở phòng triển lãm, có các buổi biểu diễn của ban nhạc và cả DJ trình diễn rap. Mục tiêu mà bảo tàng mỹ thuật Daelim hướng tới rất rõ ràng. Đó là cởi bỏ bộ mặt uy nghiêm và tạo ra một 'bảo tàng mỹ thuật thú vị'. Không giống như các bảo tàng mỹ thuật thông thường vốn hạn chế việc chụp ảnh, mọi người nói rằng bảo tàng này không mang lại cảm giác xa xôi vì bạn có thể chụp ảnh thỏa thích. Bảo tàng mỹ thuật Daelim cho thấy định hướng mà các bảo tàng mỹ thuật nên thực hiện trong tương lai.
g_text_translate.en: Daelim Museum, which is always bustling with young people, is an art museum that is more popular for its wide assortment of side events than art exhibitions. The party culture in front of Hongdae was introduced into the exhibition hall, band performances were held, and DJs performed raps. The goal that Daelim Museum aims for is clear. It is to take off the solemn face and create an 'interesting art museum'. Unlike existing art museums that limit taking pictures, people say that it is good because they can take pictures as much as they like, so the museum doesn't feel far away anymore. Daelim Museum is showing the direction museums should pursue in the future.
g_text_translate.it:
Il Daelim Museum, sempre pieno di giovani, è un museo d'arte più popolare per il suo vasto assortimento di eventi collaterali che per le mostre d'arte. La cultura della festa di fronte a Hongdae è stata introdotta nella sala espositiva, si sono svolte esibizioni di band e i DJ hanno eseguito rap. L’obiettivo che si prefigge il Daelim Museum è chiaro. Si tratta di togliersi il volto solenne e creare un 'interessante museo d'arte'. A differenza dei musei d'arte esistenti che limitano lo scatto di foto, la gente dice che è positivo perché possono scattare foto quanto vogliono, quindi il museo non sembra più lontano. Il Museo Daelim mostra la direzione che i musei dovrebbero perseguire in futuro.

#######

g_text_translate.fr:
Le musée Daelim, qui regorge toujours de jeunes, est un musée d'art plus populaire pour son large éventail d'événements parallèles que pour ses expositions d'art. La culture de la fête devant Hongdae a été introduite dans la salle d'exposition, des concerts de groupes ont eu lieu et des DJ ont joué du rap. L’objectif poursuivi par le musée Daelim est clair. Il s'agit d'enlever le visage solennel et de créer un « musée d'art intéressant ». Contrairement aux musées d'art existants qui limitent la prise de photos, les gens disent que c'est bien parce qu'ils peuvent prendre des photos autant qu'ils le souhaitent, donc le musée ne se sent plus loin. Le musée Daelim montre la direction que les musées devraient suivre à l'avenir.

いつも若者たちで賑わっている大林美術館は、美術展よりも充実したサイドイベントが人気の美術館です。弘大前のパーティー文化が展示会場に導入され、バンド演奏が行われ、DJがラップを披露した。大林博物館が目指す目標は明確です。それは、厳粛な顔を脱いで「面白い美術館」を作ることだ。写真撮影が制限されている既存の美術館と違って、好きなだけ写真を撮れるので、美術館が遠くに感じられなくなって良いと言われています。大林博物館は博物館が今後追求すべき方向性を示している。

g_text_translate.de:
Das Daelim Museum ist ein Kunstmuseum, in dem sich immer viele junge Leute tummeln und das eher wegen seiner großen Auswahl an Nebenveranstaltungen als wegen seiner Kunstausstellungen beliebt ist. Es geht darum, das feierliche Gesicht abzustreifen und ein „interessantes Kunstmuseum“ zu schaffen. Im Gegensatz zu bestehenden Kunstmuseen, die das Fotografieren einschränken, sagen die Leute, dass es gut ist, weil sie so viel fotografieren können, wie sie möchten, sodass das Museum nicht mehr weit weg wirkt. Das Daelim Museum zeigt die Richtung auf, die Museen in Zukunft einschlagen sollten.

g_text_translate.hi:
डेलीम संग्रहालय, जो हमेशा युवा लोगों से भरा रहता है, एक कला संग्रहालय है जो कला प्रदर्शनियों की तुलना में साइड इवेंट के विस्तृत वर्गीकरण के लिए अधिक लोकप्रिय है। होंगडे के सामने पार्टी संस्कृति को प्रदर्शनी हॉल में पेश किया गया, बैंड प्रदर्शन हुए और डीजे ने रैप प्रस्तुत किए। डेलीम संग्रहालय का लक्ष्य स्पष्ट है। यह गंभीर चेहरा उतारना और एक 'दिलचस्प कला संग्रहालय' बनाना है। मौजूदा कला संग्रहालयों के विपरीत, जो तस्वीरें लेने को सीमित करते हैं, लोग कहते हैं कि यह अच्छा है क्योंकि वे जितनी चाहें उतनी तस्वीरें ले सकते हैं, इसलिए संग्रहालय अब दूर नहीं लगता है। डेलीम संग्रहालय वह दिशा दिखा रहा है जिसका अनुसरण संग्रहालयों को भविष्य में करना चाहिए।

explain.vi:
Chọn suy nghĩ trọng tâm của đoạn văn.
1. Việc các bảo tàng mỹ thuật khác chấp nhận chương trình của bảo tàng mỹ thuật Daelim là điều tốt.
2. Các bảo tàng mỹ thuật cần nỗ lực tiếp cận gần với người dân.
3. Phải thú vị như bảo tàng mỹ thuật Daelim thì mới được nhiều người trẻ tìm đến.
4. Bảo tàng mỹ thuật nên chuẩn bị không chỉ các tác phẩm mỹ thuật mà còn cả các sự kiện đi kèm.
--------------------
Đoạn văn kết luận rằng bảo tàng Daelim cho thấy định hướng mà các bảo tàng mỹ thuật nên thực hiện trong tương lai. Như vậy các bảo tàng mỹ thuật cần nỗ lực tiếp cận gần với người dân như bảo tàng Daelim đã làm. 2 là đáp án đúng.

general.g_text: 인류 문명은 자연 개발과 자연 보호라는 모순 속에서 발달해 왔다. 그 중에서 인류가 소홀히 한 부분은 바로 물이다. 물의 소중함을 잊고 물을 오염시키고 만 것이다. 이에 따라 세계 각지에서 많은 사람들이 수질 오염과 물 부족으로 고통당하고 지역 간, 국가 간 물 분쟁이 끊임없이 일어나서 (        ) 있다. 이제 물 부족 문제는 한 국가의 문제만이 아니라 세계적인 문제가 되고 있다.

g_text_translate.vi: Nền văn minh nhân loại đã phát triển trong sự mâu thuẫn giữa khai thác thiên nhiên và bảo tồn thiên nhiên. Phần mà nhân loại bỏ qua trong số đó chính là nước. Họ quên mất tầm quan trọng của nước và cuối cùng đã làm ô nhiễm nó. Kết quả là nhiều người trên thế giới phải chịu cảnh ô nhiễm nước và thiếu nước, tranh chấp nước giữa các vùng và các quốc gia liên tục xảy ra nên đang (        ). Khan hiếm nước hiện đang là vấn đề toàn cầu, không chỉ là vấn đề quốc gia.
g_text_translate.en: Human civilization has developed in the contradiction of natural development and nature conservation. Among them, the part that mankind has neglected is water. They forgot the preciousness of water and polluted it. As a result, many people around the world are suffering from water pollution and water shortages, and water disputes between regions and countries are constantly occurring, causing headaches. Now, the water shortage problem is not only a national problem, but a global problem.
g_text_translate.it:
La civiltà umana si è sviluppata nella contraddizione tra sviluppo naturale e conservazione della natura. Tra questi, la parte che l’umanità ha trascurato è l’acqua. Hanno dimenticato la preziosità dell’acqua e l’hanno inquinata. Di conseguenza, molte persone in tutto il mondo soffrono di inquinamento e carenza idrica e le controversie idriche tra regioni e paesi si verificano costantemente, causando mal di testa. Ora, il problema della carenza idrica non è solo un problema nazionale, ma un problema globale.

#######

Human civilization has developed in the contradiction of natural development and nature conservation. Among them, the part that mankind has neglected is water. They forgot the preciousness of water and polluted it. As a result, many people around the world are suffering from water pollution and water shortages, and water disputes between regions and countries are constantly occurring, causing headaches. Now, the water shortage problem is not only a national problem, but a global problem.
g_text_translate.fr:
La civilisation humaine s’est développée dans une contradiction entre le développement naturel et la conservation de la nature. Parmi eux, la partie que l’humanité a négligée est l’eau. Ils ont oublié le caractère précieux de l’eau et l’ont polluée. En conséquence, de nombreuses personnes dans le monde souffrent de la pollution de l’eau et de pénuries d’eau, et des conflits sur l’eau entre régions et pays surviennent constamment, provoquant des maux de tête. Aujourd’hui, le problème de la pénurie d’eau n’est pas seulement un problème national, mais un problème mondial.

#######

g_text_translate.es:
La civilización humana se ha desarrollado en contradicción entre el desarrollo natural y la conservación de la naturaleza. Entre ellos, la parte que la humanidad ha descuidado es el agua. Se olvidaron del valor del agua y la contaminaron. Como resultado, muchas personas en todo el mundo sufren contaminación y escasez de agua, y constantemente ocurren disputas por el agua entre regiones y países, lo que causa dolores de cabeza. Ahora, el problema de la escasez de agua no es sólo un problema nacional, sino un problema global.

g_text_translate.id:
Peradaban manusia berkembang dalam kontradiksi antara pembangunan alam dan pelestarian alam. Diantaranya, bagian yang diabaikan umat manusia adalah air. Mereka melupakan betapa berharganya air dan mencemarinya. Akibatnya, banyak orang di seluruh dunia menderita polusi air dan kekurangan air, dan perselisihan air antar wilayah dan negara terus terjadi sehingga menyebabkan sakit kepala. Kini permasalahan kekurangan air bukan hanya menjadi permasalahan nasional, namun menjadi permasalahan global.

g_text_translate.ja:
人類の文明は、自然の発展と自然の保護という矛盾の中で発展してきました。その中でも人類が軽視してきたのが「水」です。彼らは水の大切さを忘れ、水を汚してしまいました。その結果、世界中で多くの人が水質汚染や水不足に苦しんでおり、地域や国家間の水紛争も絶えず発生しており、頭を悩ませています。今や水不足問題は国内だけの問題ではなく、世界的な問題となっています。

g_text_translate.de:
Die menschliche Zivilisation hat sich im Widerspruch zwischen natürlicher Entwicklung und Naturschutz entwickelt. Unter ihnen ist Wasser der Teil, den die Menschheit vernachlässigt hat. Sie vergaßen die Kostbarkeit des Wassers und verschmutzten es. Dadurch leiden weltweit viele Menschen unter Wasserverschmutzung und Wasserknappheit, und es kommt immer wieder zu Wasserstreitigkeiten zwischen Regionen und Ländern, die für Kopfzerbrechen sorgen. Nun ist das Problem der Wasserknappheit nicht nur ein nationales Problem, sondern ein globales Problem.

g_text_translate.hi:
प्राकृतिक विकास और प्रकृति संरक्षण के विरोधाभास में मानव सभ्यता का विकास हुआ है। इनमें से जिस हिस्से को मानव जाति ने उपेक्षित किया है वह है पानी। वे जल की महत्ता को भूल गये और उसे प्रदूषित कर दिया। परिणामस्वरूप, दुनिया भर में कई लोग जल प्रदूषण और पानी की कमी से पीड़ित हैं, और क्षेत्रों और देशों के बीच जल विवाद लगातार हो रहे हैं, जिससे सिरदर्द हो रहा है। अब जल की कमी की समस्या एक राष्ट्रीय समस्या नहीं, बल्कि एक वैश्विक समस्या बन गयी है।

Bunun sonucunda dünya çapında pek çok insan su kirliliği ve su kıtlığı yaşamakta, bölgeler ve ülkeler arasında sürekli su anlaşmazlıkları yaşanmakta ve baş ağrılarına neden olmaktadır. q_answer: 인류는 물의 소중함을 잊고 물을 오염시켰다.
인류 문명은 물과 함께 성장해 왔다.
물 부족 현상을 대비하여 물을 아껴 써야 한다.
물 부족 문제는 모든 국가의 문제가 되었다.

explain.vi:
Chọn suy nghĩ trọng tâm của đoạn văn.
1. Nhân loại đã quên mất tầm quan trọng của nước và làm ô nhiễm nước. 
2. Nền văn minh nhân loại đã phát triển cùng với nước. 
3. Phải tiết kiệm nước để đề phòng tình trạng thiếu nước.
4. Vấn đề thiếu nước đã trở thành vấn đề của tất cả các quốc gia.
--------------------

general.g_text: 축구 선수 11명이 운동장을 누벼도 시야가 넓은 선수는 운동장 전체를 보기 때문에 어디가 비어 있고 어디로 공을 보내야 좋을지 잘 볼 수 있다. 이런 선수는 힘을 덜 들이고 효과적인 축구를 한다. 그러나 시야가 좁은 선수는 운동장의 한 부분만을 보기 때문에 항상 이미 수비진이 지키고 있는 곳을 뚫기 위해 (        ) 실패만 거듭한다. 우리의 인생도 비슷하다. 따라서 넓게 볼 수 있을 때 삶을 성공적으로 살아갈 수 있다.

g_text_translate.vi: Ngay cả khi 11 cầu thủ bóng đá chạy khắp sân vận động, cầu thủ có tầm nhìn rộng vẫn nhìn thấy toàn bộ sân, vì vậy họ có thể thấy rõ chỗ nào trống và nên đá bóng đến đâu thì tốt. Những cầu thủ này ít tốn sức hơn và chơi bóng đá hiệu quả. Tuy nhiên, các cầu thủ có tầm nhìn hẹp chỉ nhìn thấy một phần của sân, vì vậy họ (        ) để xuyên thủng những nơi mà hậu vệ canh giữ nhưng đều thất bại. Cuộc sống của chúng ta cũng giống như vậy. Do đó, bạn có thể sống cuộc sống thành công khi bạn có thể nhìn rộng.
g_text_translate.en: Even if 11 soccer players walk across the field, a player with a wide field of view can see where the field is empty and where to send the ball. Such a player takes less effort and plays effective soccer. However, a player with a narrow field of view sees only one part of the field, so he always fails while sweating to penetrate the place already guarded by the defense. Our lives are similar. Therefore, when you can see broadly, you can successfully live life.
g_text_translate.it:
Anche se 11 giocatori di calcio attraversano il campo, un giocatore con un ampio campo visivo può vedere dove il campo è vuoto e dove mandare la palla. Un giocatore del genere impiega meno sforzo e gioca un calcio efficace. Tuttavia, un giocatore con un campo visivo ristretto vede solo una parte del campo, quindi sudando non riesce sempre a penetrare nel punto già presidiato dalla difesa. Le nostre vite sono simili. Pertanto, quando riesci a vedere in modo ampio, puoi vivere la vita con successo.

#######

Even if 11 soccer players walk across the field, a player with a wide field of view can see where the field is empty and where to send the ball. Such a player takes less effort and plays effective soccer. However, a player with a narrow field of view sees only one part of the field, so he always fails while sweating to penetrate the place already guarded by the defense. Our lives are similar. Therefore, when you can see broadly, you can successfully live life.
g_text_translate.fr:
Même si 11 footballeurs traversent le terrain, un joueur disposant d’un large champ de vision peut voir où le terrain est vide et où envoyer le ballon. Un tel joueur demande moins d’efforts et joue un football efficace. Cependant, un joueur avec un champ de vision étroit ne voit qu'une partie du terrain, il échoue donc toujours en transpirant à pénétrer dans l'endroit déjà gardé par la défense. Nos vies sont similaires. Par conséquent, lorsque vous pouvez voir largement, vous pouvez vivre votre vie avec succès.

#######

Even if 11 soccer players walk across the field, a player with a wide field of view can see where the field is empty and where to send the ball. Such a player takes less effort and plays effective soccer. However, a player with a narrow field of view sees only one part of the field, so he always fails while sweating to penetrate the place already guarded by the defense. Our lives are similar. Therefore, when you can see broadly, you can successfully live life.
g_text_translate.es:
Incluso si 11 jugadores de fútbol caminan por el campo, un jugador con un campo de visión amplio puede ver dónde está vacío el campo y hacia dónde enviar el balón. Un jugador así requiere menos esfuerzo y juega un fútbol eficaz. Sin embargo, un jugador con un campo de visión estrecho sólo ve una parte del campo, por lo que, sudando, siempre no logra penetrar el lugar ya protegido por la defensa. Nuestras vidas son similares. Por lo tanto, cuando puedes ver con amplitud, puedes vivir la vida con éxito.

#######

Even if 11 soccer players walk across the field, a player with a wide field of view can see where the field is empty and where to send the ball. Such a player takes less effort and plays effective soccer. However, a player with a narrow field of view sees only one part of the field, so he always fails while sweating to penetrate the place already guarded by the defense. Our lives are similar. Therefore, when you can see broadly, you can successfully live life.
g_text_translate.id:
Bahkan jika 11 pemain sepak bola berjalan melintasi lapangan, pemain dengan bidang pandang yang luas dapat melihat di mana lapangan kosong dan ke mana harus mengirim bola. Pemain seperti itu membutuhkan lebih sedikit usaha dan memainkan sepak bola yang efektif. Namun pemain dengan jarak pandang yang sempit hanya melihat satu bagian lapangan saja sehingga selalu gagal sambil berkeringat untuk menembus tempat yang sudah dijaga oleh pertahanan. Kehidupan kami serupa. Oleh karena itu, ketika Anda bisa melihat secara luas, Anda bisa sukses menjalani hidup.

#######

Even if 11 soccer players walk across the field, a player with a wide field of view can see where the field is empty and where to send the ball. Such a player takes less effort and plays effective soccer. However, a player with a narrow field of view sees only one part of the field, so he always fails while sweating to penetrate the place already guarded by the defense. Our lives are similar. Therefore, when you can see broadly, you can successfully live life.
g_text_translate.ja:
たとえ 11 人のサッカー選手がフィールドを横切って歩いても、広い視野を持つ選手であれば、フィールドのどこに人がいないのか、どこにボールを送ればよいのかがわかります。そのような選手は労力が少なく、効果的なサッカーをします。しかし、視野が狭い選手はフィールドの一部分しか見ていないため、すでにディフェンスに守られている場所への突破は汗だくになりながらいつも失敗してしまいます。私たちの生活も似ています。したがって、広い視野を持つことができれば、人生をうまく生きることができます。

#######

Even if 11 soccer players walk across the field, a player with a wide field of view can see where the field is empty and where to send the ball. Such a player takes less effort and plays effective soccer. However, a player with a narrow field of view sees only one part of the field, so he always fails while sweating to penetrate the place already guarded by the defense. Our lives are similar. Therefore, when you can see broadly, you can successfully live life.
g_text_translate.de:
Selbst wenn 11 Fußballspieler über das Spielfeld laufen, kann ein Spieler mit weitem Sichtfeld sehen, wo das Spielfeld leer ist und wohin er den Ball schicken muss. Ein solcher Spieler erfordert weniger Anstrengung und spielt effektiven Fußball. Allerdings sieht ein Spieler mit einem engen Sichtfeld nur einen Teil des Feldes, so dass es ihm schwitzend immer nicht gelingt, in den von der Verteidigung bereits bewachten Bereich einzudringen. Unser Leben ist ähnlich. Wenn Sie also weitblicken können, können Sie Ihr Leben erfolgreich führen.

#######

Even if 11 soccer players walk across the field, a player with a wide field of view can see where the field is empty and where to send the ball. Such a player takes less effort and plays effective soccer. However, a player with a narrow field of view sees only one part of the field, so he always fails while sweating to penetrate the place already guarded by the defense. Our lives are similar. Therefore, when you can see broadly, you can successfully live life.
g_text_translate.zh-CN:
即使 11 名足球运动员走过球场，视野开阔的球员也能看到球场上哪里是空的以及将球传到哪里。这样的球员花费更少的力气就能踢出有效的足球。然而，视野狭窄的球员只能看到球场的一部分，所以他总是在汗流浃背的情况下无法突破防守者已经把守的地方。我们的生活很相似。因此，当你的视野开阔时，你就能成功地度过人生。

Даже если по полю пройдут 11 футболистов, игрок с широким полем зрения сможет увидеть, где поле пусто и куда направить мяч. Такой игрок требует меньше усилий и играет в результативный футбол. g_text_translate.th:
แม้ว่านักฟุตบอล 11 คนจะเดินข้ามสนาม ผู้เล่นที่มีขอบเขตการมองเห็นกว้างจะสามารถมองเห็นตำแหน่งที่สนามว่างและตำแหน่งที่จะส่งบอลได้ นักเตะแบบนี้ใช้ความพยายามน้อยลงและเล่นฟุตบอลได้อย่างมีประสิทธิภาพ อย่างไรก็ตาม ผู้เล่นที่มีมุมมองแคบจะมองเห็นเพียงส่วนหนึ่งของสนาม ดังนั้นเขาจึงมักจะล้มเหลวในขณะที่พยายามอย่างหนักเพื่อเจาะเข้าไปในพื้นที่ที่มีการป้องกันอยู่แล้ว ชีวิตเราก็คล้ายกัน ดังนั้นเมื่อมองให้กว้างไกลก็ดำเนินชีวิตได้สำเร็จ

#######

Even if 11 soccer players walk across the field, a player with a wide field of view can see where the field is empty and where to send the ball. Such a player takes less effort and plays effective soccer. However, a player with a narrow field of view sees only one part of the field, so he always fails while sweating to penetrate the place already guarded by the defense. Our lives are similar. Therefore, when you can see broadly, you can successfully live life.
g_text_translate.pt:
Mesmo que 11 jogadores de futebol atravessem o campo, um jogador com amplo campo de visão pode ver onde o campo está vazio e para onde enviar a bola. Esse jogador exige menos esforço e joga um futebol eficaz. Porém, um jogador com campo de visão estreito vê apenas uma parte do campo, por isso sempre falha ao suar para penetrar no local já guardado pela defesa. Nossas vidas são semelhantes. Portanto, quando você consegue ver de forma ampla, você pode viver a vida com sucesso.

#######

g_text_translate.hi:
यहां तक ​​कि अगर 11 फुटबॉल खिलाड़ी मैदान के पार चलते हैं, तो व्यापक दृश्य वाला खिलाड़ी देख सकता है कि मैदान कहां खाली है और गेंद को कहां भेजना है। ऐसा खिलाड़ी कम मेहनत करता है और प्रभावी फुटबॉल खेलता है। हालाँकि, एक संकीर्ण दृश्य क्षेत्र वाला खिलाड़ी मैदान का केवल एक ही हिस्सा देखता है, इसलिए वह पहले से ही रक्षा द्वारा संरक्षित जगह को भेदने में पसीना बहाते हुए हमेशा विफल रहता है। हमारा जीवन एक जैसा है. इसलिए, जब आप व्यापक रूप से देख सकते हैं, तो आप सफलतापूर्वक जीवन जी सकते हैं।

g_text_translate.tr:
Sahada 11 futbolcu yürüse bile geniş görüş alanına sahip bir oyuncu sahanın neresinin boş olduğunu, topu nereye atacağını görebilir. Böyle bir oyuncu daha az çaba harcar ve etkili futbol oynar. Ancak dar görüş alanına sahip bir oyuncu, sahanın yalnızca bir kısmını görür, bu nedenle ter dökerken savunmanın zaten koruduğu yere girmeyi her zaman başaramaz. Hayatlarımız benzer. Dolayısıyla geniş bakabildiğinizde hayatı başarılı bir şekilde yaşayabilirsiniz.

--------------------
